title: Training and Seminar Held for the Academic Staff of the Faculty of Education
content: Training and Seminar Held for Academic Staff of Faculty of Education 13.01.2025 Upon request of Faculty of Education, IZU CELT organized a training session and seminar on January 13, 2025, to enhance professional competence of faculty’s academic staff. As part of program, Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kamil Arif Kirkiç delivered two key events. The training session titled “Writing Learning Outcomes in Higher Education” focused on defining learning outcomes, formulating learning objectives, and understanding Turkish Higher Education Qualifications Framework through an interactive format. In seminar “The Importance of CELTs”, participants were introduced to purpose and significance of Teaching and Learning Centers for academic development.

title: A Protocol Signed Between IZÜ and MÜSIAD
content: A Protocol Signed Between IZÜ MÜSIAD 29.01.2025 January 15, 2025 The President of MÜSIAD’s Digital Transformation Sector Board, Mr. Taha Çiftçi, along with an accompanying delegation, paid a visit to our university. Hosted by IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, visit culminated in signing of an education cooperation protocol between MÜSIAD our university. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University had privilege of welcoming members of Independent Industrialists’ Businessmen’s Association (MÜSIAD) Digital Transformation Sector Board. Under leadership of Board President Taha Çiftçi, delegation toured Faculty of Engineering Natural Sciences laboratories. The visitors were introduced to Pardus Laboratories, established in collaboration with TÜBITAK, where significance of open-source ecosystem was emphasized. Discussions also focused on ongoing efforts to develop domestic national technological systems. Furthermore, delegation was briefed on research conducted at Sabri Ülker Halal Food Laboratory. Following laboratory visits, delegation proceeded to Teknopark, where they engaged in discussions with our academics Teknopark administrators. The meeting, attended by Prof. Dr. Tarik Özkul, Dr. Sengül Bayrak Hayta, Dr. Oktay Dogangün, Dr. Artrim Kjamilji, Dr. Hakan Gençoglu, TTO Director Aysenur Yalçin, provided a platform for evaluating current projects exploring potential areas of collaboration. NEW OPPORTUNITIES FOR IZÜ STUDENTS THROUGH THE IYMEP PROTOCOL During visit, an Education Cooperation Protocol was formally signed between our university MÜSIAD. Additionally, in collaboration with MÜSIAD Digital Transformation Sector Board members, Workplace Vocational Training Program (IYMEP) Protocol was also concluded. Under this agreement, IZÜ students who meet required criteria will have opportunity to complete their entire eighth semester in a professional workplace environment , allowing them to gain hands-on industry experience.

title: IZU Students Exhibited Their Works
content: IZU Students Exhibited Their Works 30.10.2024 The "Basic Art Education and Analytical Design" exhibition, featuring the works of IZU students, was inaugurated by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University hosted the "Basic Art Education and Analytical Design" exhibition organized by the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences, Department of Visual Communication Design. The exhibition featured works by IZU students, presenting modern design techniques alongside portraits and abstract drawings for visitors to enjoy. IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar and Faculty Dean Prof. Dr. Mehmet Emin Köktas examined the students' drawings and received information about their work. Led by Dr. Hasan Çevik, Head of the Visual Communication Design Department, the exhibition also included guidance from Dr. Ahmet Doksanoglu, Dr. Recep Çökerdenoglu, and Research Assistant Ugur Karabulut, who contributed to shaping the students' creations.

title: Countless Opportunities in Transformation, No Barriers to Learning
content: Countless Opportunities in Transformation, No Barriers to Learning 29.01.2025 January 17, 2025 The Faculty of Education at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University hosted two exhibitions: "Countless Opportunities in Transformation, No Barriers to Learning" and "Sensory Materials in Early Childhood". Held in faculty’s foyer area, exhibitions showcased materials designed by students from Special Education Teaching and Preschool Teaching programs. The opening of "Sensory Materials in Early Childhood" exhibition was attended by IZÜ Board of Trustees Chairman Özkan Göksal, IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector and Dean of Faculty of Education Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, and Vice Dean Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kâmil Arif Kirkiç. The "Countless Opportunities in Transformation, No Barriers to Learning" exhibition was attended by Vice Rector and Dean of Faculty of Education Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Head of Special Education Department Assoc. Prof. Dr. Nüket Afat, Deputy Head of Department Dr. Adnan Ari, along with academics and numerous students. The exhibition featured materials developed by fourth-year Special Education Teaching students as part of course "OZE 401: Material Design in Special Education." The primary goal was to support learning through recycling while contributing to special education. Visitors showed great interest in game-based educational materials designed from recyclable materials, aimed at teaching academic skills such as literacy and mathematics .

title: The “BasAri Kadin” Program Shined a Light on Women's Entrepreneurship
content: The “BasAri Kadin” Program Shined a Light on Women's Entrepreneurship 30.10.2024 The “BasAri Kadin” program, organized by Entrepreneurship Club, was held in Museum Hall with participation of our Rector, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar. The event focused on role of women in business world featured speakers from IZU, including Dr. Tugçenur Ekinci Furtana, Lecturer in International Trade Finance Department, SKS Department Head Hülya Sekerci. Also speaking were Entrepreneur Kübra Orakçioglu Kazan, GZT Editor Kevser Erbay, Lokma Digital Publishing Director Beyza Nur Sahin. The “BasAri Kadin” program, organized by IZU’s Entrepreneurship Club, took place with attendance of our Rector, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar. The event included notable speakers in women’s entrepreneurship, such as IZU’s International Trade Finance Lecturer Dr. Tugçenur Ekinci Furtana SKS Department Head Hülya Sekerci. In his opening remarks, Rector Acar highlighted growing presence of women in business world, emphasizing importance of female entrepreneurs female workforce in Turkey’s economy. He stated, “Today, women play a more active role in business world. Most of our university students are women. With increased competition, Turkish economy remains dependent on external sources. We don’t have oil, so women play a vital role in revealing innovative spirit of Turkey's economy. Successful women will speak here today, their stories will inspire you.” Dr. Tugçenur Ekinci Furtana expressed her pleasure in attending as an entrepreneur, adding, “Our aim is to reach all women in field of entrepreneurship under our university’s roof.” Entrepreneur Kübra Orakçioglu Kazan shared her experiences in business world, while GZT Editor Kevser Erbay Lokma Digital Publishing Director Beyza Nur Sahin discussed increasing role of women in digital media.

title: An Innovative Approach to Language Teaching from IZU
content: An Innovative Approach to Language Teaching from IZU 30.10.2024 Our faculty member Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kâmil Arif Kirkiç and IZU master's graduate Dilara Saygin presented their study on impact of digital platforms in language learning at CALICO 2024 Conference in United States. The research explored innovative approaches to effects of artificial intelligence and virtual reality technologies on language education. Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kâmil Arif Kirkiç from Faculty of Education at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University and IZU English Education master's graduate Dilara Saygin presented their study, titled “Voxy as an LXP: How Does it Affect Language Learning in Classroom? Is it a Myth or Reality?” at CALICO (Computer-Assisted Language Instruction Consortium) 2024 conference held at Carnegie Mellon University (CMU) in United States. The conference highlighted innovations in language education brought by technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), and extended reality (XR). CALICO aims to share latest advancements and applications in computer-assisted language learning (CALL), showcasing contributions of these technologies to language teaching. The presentation, based on a master’s thesis under Dr. Kirkiç’s supervision, examined opportunities technology offers in language instruction, specifically analyzing impact of digital learning experience platform “Voxy” (LXP) on classroom language learning.

title: IZU Students Completed Their First Aid Training
content: IZU Students Completed Their First Aid Training 30.10.2024 The “First Aid Training” project, organized for prospective teachers at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University's Faculty of Education, has been successfully completed. Led by Dr. Ayse Isik Öner from the Department of Primary Education, the project (code 2024-BAP-400-005) included Research Assistant Çagla Bingül and student Merve Nisa Ekmekçi as researchers. After comprehensive first aid training provided by AKUT, participating teacher candidates qualified to receive certification by passing written and practical exams administered by the Ministry of Health.

title: The Faculty of Sports Sciences Academic Council Meeting Took Place
content: The Faculty of Sports Sciences Academic Council Meeting Took Place 30.10.2024 The Faculty of Sports Sciences Academic Council Meeting for the 2024-2025 Fall Semester was held in the Young Office Conference Hall at IZU. The meeting was attended by Faculty Dean Prof. Dr. Mustafa Zahit Serarslan, Vice Dean Dr. Abdusselam Köse, and other faculty members. Dean Serarslan provided participants with updates on department status, faculty staffing, student advising, academic orientation programs, and publication activities. title: New Semester Excitement at IZU Faculty of Sports Sciences
content: New Semester Excitement at IZU Faculty of Sports Sciences 30.10.2024 The 2024-2025 Academic Year Orientation Program for Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University’s Faculty of Sports Sciences was held in the Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. During the program, comprehensive information was provided to students who enrolled based on the Special Talent Exam (ÖZYES) results. The orientation program included participation from Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Faculty of Sports Sciences Dean Prof. Dr. Mustafa Zahit Serarslan, as well as faculty members and students. Moderated by Vice Dean Dr. Abdusselam Köse, the program began with an opening speech by Dean Prof. Dr. Mustafa Zahit Serarslan. Dean Serarslan emphasized that university education goes beyond academic courses, urging students to focus on their career planning during their four years of study. Vice Rector Güney highlighted IZU’s rising status among Turkey’s esteemed institutions due to its advancing educational quality, noting that the university’s sports facilities provide students with a significant advantage for their personal growth. The program also introduced the faculty’s academic and administrative staff, while offering students guidance on topics such as course registration and exam weeks.

title: Support from IZU for the “From the Past to the Future: Ahlat” Project
content: Support from IZU for “From Past to Future: Ahlat” Project 30.10.2024 Our faculty member Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kâmil Arif Kirkiç gave a seminar to students as part of "From Past to Future: Ahlat" project organized by Ministry of National Education. Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kâmil Arif Kirkiç, a faculty member from Department of Educational Sciences at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, was among invited speakers for "From Past to Future: Ahlat" project organized by Ministry of National Education at Presidential Complex in Ahlat, Bitlis. As part of project, Kirkiç delivered seminars on "Leadership and Entrepreneurship" to two groups comprising 176 students and 22 teachers. These seminars took place at Sadullah Gencer Anatolian High School, Selçuklu Anatolian High School, and Ali Ayber Anatolian High School. The project was implemented with support from Ministry of National Education Advisor Süleyman Can, MEB Department Head Abdülhalik Bas, Ahlat District Governor Batuhan Bingöl, Ahlat Mayor Yavuz Gülmez, and District Education Director Rafet Akgün.

title: Signatures Signed at the International Knowledge Conference
content: Signatures Signed at the International Knowledge Conference 30.10.2024 As part of the “17th International Knowledge, Economy, Management Conference,” a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) was signed between Tashkent University Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. The agreement, attended by Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar Prof. Dr. Teshaboyev To’lqin Zokiroviç, aims to strengthen relations between the two universities establish a foundation for high-quality collaborative projects. The "17th International Knowledge, Economy, Management Conference" was held in collaboration with Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, Tashkent State University of Economics, Mara University of Technology. The conference, hosted in Uzbekistan’s capital Tashkent, included IZU representatives such as Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Dean of the Faculty of Business Management Sciences Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güran Yumusak, as well as other IZU academics. As part of the program, a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) was signed between Tashkent University Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. This agreement, attended by Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar Prof. Dr. Teshaboyev To’lqin Zokiroviç, aims to enhance cultural academic interaction through student exchange programs, joint research projects, collaborative workshops, shared conferences.

title: The phrase translates to "The Distance or Contrast Between Religion and Politics: The Result of Modernism
content: The phrase translates to "The Distance or Contrast Between Religion Politics: The Result of Modernism 09.10.2024 At seminar titled "Modern Images of Relationship Between Religion Politics," organized by our Political Science Club, our faculty member from Islamic Studies Department, Ismail Kara, stated that distance or contrast between politics is a result of modernism. The seminar titled "Modern Images of Relationship Between Religion Politics," featuring Ismail Kara from Islamic Studies Department at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University as a speaker, was successfully held. The event was attended by university's Rector, Ahmet Cevat Acar; Vice Rector, Erhan Içener; Dean of Faculty of Humanities Social Sciences, Mehmet Emin Köktas. "It is impossible for social values to disregard religion." In opening speeches of program, IZÜ Vice Rector Erhan Içener emphasized importance of relationship between politics in social sphere, stating, "It is an honor for us to have Ismail Kara as part of our Political Science Club's first program." IZÜ Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar highlighted role of in social values. He stated, "We must stay away from prejudices. It is clear that is an indispensable element. It is impossible for social values to disregard religion. Polarization has led us to become a society that engages in unproductive debates. We should evaluate relationship between politics primarily from an objective perspective." "The contrast between politics is a result of modernism." Ismail Kara from Islamic Studies Department at IZÜ explained how distinction between politics has developed in a historical context during his seminar titled "Modern Images of Relationship Between Religion Politics." He stated, "The relationship between politics is a contemporary issue. Before modern era, it was quite difficult to understand separation of politics. The increasing distance between them has led to a contrast rupture, which is a result of modernism. To understand modernization, three key points should be addressed: First, we must discuss modern science philosophy, which are important elements of Enlightenment philosophy. Second, processes of secularism secularization are crucial. Third, capitalism forms underlying thought structure of our daily lifestyle. In this context, capitalism has nothing to do with appearance of freedom." After seminar, participants had opportunity to deeply discuss effects of modernization on relationship between politics.

title: 1. The National CBRN Congress Was Held at IZÜ.
content: 1. The National Congress Was Held at IZÜ. 20.11.2024 The "1st National Congress" themed "Preparedness for Chemical, Biological, Radiological, Nuclear (CBRN) Threats," organized by IZÜ Faculty of Health Sciences, Department of Nursing, was held. The congress focused on discussing measures to be taken against threats in field of healthcare. The "1st National Congress," organized by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Faculty of Health Sciences, Department of Nursing, was held at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The program, themed "Preparedness for Chemical, Biological, Radiological, Nuclear (CBRN) Threats," was attended by Ayse Nefise Bahçecik, Dean of IZÜ Faculty of Health Sciences; Levent Kenar, President of Defense Policy Development Association; numerous academics students. In her opening speech, Dean Bahçecik emphasized that purpose of event was to share knowledge experiences about threats. She stated, "CBRN threats involve complex challenges, overcoming these risks requires collaboration among many actors from public sector, private sector, academia, civil society." Levent Kenar, President of Defense Policy Development Association, highlighted that weapons have serious consequences not only in military contexts but also in civilian life. He stressed critical importance of healthcare workers being prepared for these threats noted that congress would contribute to development of effective strategies. ADDRESSED FROM A MULTIDIMENSIONAL PERSPECTIVE In first session of program, experts delivered presentations on threats. Levent Kenar provided insights into chemical threats, Assoc. Gürsel Karahan discussed radiological incidents, Mustafa Nizamettin Erduran covered nuclear threats. Assoc. Mesut Ortatatli delivered a comprehensive presentation on bioterrorism. The second session focused on preparedness for threats. Samet Kiliç, Istanbul UMKE Coordinator from Ministry of Health, explained medical rescue processes. Seval Dedoglu from Istanbul AFAD Unit Directorate provided information about Turkey's disaster response plan. Additionally, Sefer Küçük, Istanbul AFAD Team Commander, conducted a demonstration on response processes for incidents. The final session addressed management in emergency healthcare services. Malik Türkay Esin, Deputy Chief Physician of Taksim Training Research Hospital, shared insights on impact on emergency healthcare services. Assoc. Mesut Ortatatli provided information on a hospital decontamination unit as an example, while Assoc. Aynur Sahin discussed clinical syndromes observed in toxic terrorism emergency management strategies.

title: The Voice of Palestine Echoed at IZÜ
content: The Voice of Palestine Echoed at IZÜ 09.10.2024 The program titled "In 837th Year of Glorious Conquest: The Land of Witnesses, Palestine," organized by Islamic Studies Club Ahdimiz Kudüs Platform, was held at IZÜ. In his opening speech, our Dean of Islamic Studies Department, Prof. Dr. Hasan Kâmil Yilmaz, stated that anger against oppression in Palestine needs to increase. The program titled "In 837th Year of Glorious Conquest: The Land of Witnesses, Palestine," held at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, drew attention to ongoing humanitarian crisis in Palestine. Organized by IZÜ Islamic Studies Club Ahdimiz Kudüs Platform, event took place in Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The program began with a recitation of Quran, followed by an opening speech from Prof. Dr. Hasan Kâmil Yilmaz, Dean of Islamic Studies Department at IZÜ. Dean Yilmaz emphasized need for increased anger against oppression in Palestine, stating, "The fire in our hearts grows stronger every day. A brutality leading to death of children continues. We are concerned about normalization of this situation. Our country has always stood by oppressed will continue to bear this responsibility. Our hope is for Israel's cruel stance to end for this bloody process to cease. Countries in European Union, especially United States, must take action to stop Israel. We, too, must increase our anger against Israel. Our anger needs to grow to counter this oppressive attitude." "We are praying for bloodshed to stop." Writer Bülent Deniz, who conveyed spirit of Jerusalem suffering endured by oppressed Muslims in Palestine, drew attention to Israel's attacks. He stated, "A year has passed, our oppressed brothers sisters are under attack not only from Zionist Israel but also from global colonizers. They are in need of a morsel of bread a sip of water. By slaughtering innocent children, they have chosen a dark path of submission. This is not just a struggle for land; it is a great atrocity unfolding before our eyes. We have a historical responsibility to stand by oppressed. We are praying for end of Israel's cruel stance for bloodshed to stop."

title: The Conference on the Artificial Intelligence of Objects was held at IZÜ
content: The Conference on Artificial Intelligence of Objects was held at IZÜ 09.10.2024 The "3rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence of Objects (ICAIoT 2024)" was held at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. In his opening speech, our Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük stated, "The intelligence of objects (AIoT) inspires all of you to engage in groundbreaking research innovative applications in production, logistics, healthcare, finance, energy, many other fields. The aim of this conference is to highlight transformative role of AIoT to demonstrate how it is shaping technological innovation this year." The "3rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence of Objects (ICAIoT 2024)" took place in Seminar Room 2 at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. The event brought together researchers professionals to discuss innovative applications, attended by IZÜ Vice Rector Dean of Faculty of Engineering Natural Sciences, Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, along with academics students. In his opening speech, Prof. Küçük stated that technology of object intelligence (AIoT) is leading groundbreaking innovations in various sectors such as production, logistics, healthcare, finance, energy. He said, "The aim of this conference is to highlight transformative role of AIoT to demonstrate how it is shaping technological innovation this year. We take pride in representing technology innovation in best possible way in establishing collaborations that will continue after conference. This success would not have been possible without your hard work education. We are excited to see your contributions look forward to productive discussions collaborations in future. ICAIoT 2024 will be an enjoyable educational experience for all of us. Thank you for your contributions to innovation growth, I wish you all a successful productive conference."

title: Gathered for Palestine on the Anniversary of the Gaza Occupation
content: Gathered on Anniversary of Gaza Occupation 09.10.2024 Role of in Future of Islamic World was Discussed "4th International Conference," organized by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, brought together world-renowned thinkers, academics, politicians in Istanbul. Held on anniversary of attacks, conference focused on Palestine's role in shaping future of Islamic world. Center Islamic World Global Relations Application Research (CIGA) at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University organized an important conference on Palestine, which has been subjected to Israeli attacks since October of last year. On anniversary of attacks, renowned thinkers, academics, politicians gathered in Istanbul. "4th International Conference" featured first two days of program at Kaya Istanbul Fair Congress Center, with final day taking place at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. role of in future of Islamic world was assessed through discussions on first day. World-Famous Thinkers Gathered in Istanbul. program, which began with a recitation of Quran, featured opening speeches by Abdel Razzaq Maqri, Secretary General of Kuala Lumpur Forum Thought Civilization; Sheikh Hassan Ould Al-Dadou, Vice President of Kuala Lumpur Forum Thought Civilization; Issam Al-Besir, Vice President of International Union of Muslim Scholars; Mehmet Güney, Honorary President of Human Civilization Movement; Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Rector of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. "This is a humanitarian issue beyond a religious problem." Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Rector of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, stated that a genocide is occurring in Palestine. He noted that Palestinians—children, women, men, sick, elderly—are subjected to horrific massacres. Acar said, "Humanity is watching this genocide live on TV social media. We seen once again who controls organizations like United Nations. Differences in sect, race, language, or tribe should not hinder brotherhood. Our brothers in Gaza conquered their fear. May Allah accept their martyrdom. Let us also acknowledge bigger picture. Our brothers continue to suffer oppression in various parts of world. so-called civilized West is secularizing to extent that Islamic world is unaware. We observed that not only Islamic countries, but everyone who carries human dignity is trying to offer support. This is a humanitarian issue beyond a religious problem." "We will liberate Palestine." Abdel Razzaq Maqri, Secretary General of Kuala Lumpur Forum Thought Civilization, made significant statements regarding rising awakening movements need reform around world. He emphasized that "Israel's oppressive regimes begun to weaken. public's objection to Zionism has grown," highlighting changing political atmosphere. Maqri stressed importance of acting in accordance with thought of Islamic civilization, stating, "We must conduct research collectively. We need to enter a process of self-reflection. To elevate our Ummah, we will move toward common ground. This is how we will liberate Palestine. is a unifying cause. We are in final phase of freedom." "We freed ourselves from cowardice." Sheikh Hassan Ould Al-Dadou, Vice President of Kuala Lumpur Forum Thought Civilization, stated that a new resistance movement is emerging today. He noted, "Cowardice has been a burden carried by our Ummah many years. We freed ourselves from cowardice. We started to express our ideas freely. We are in a battle against an open war on Islam. Our mujahideen sacrificed their lives. Our martyrs been sacrificed this storm. We need everyone's support," calling solidarity. "They are trying to make us forget cause." Issam Al-Besir, Vice President of International Union of Muslim Scholars, emphasized importance of social unity resistance. He stated, "For a long time, there has been fear over Ummah. Our religion tells us that we must stand against this Zionist entity. It is important all of us to gather in one place say no to sectarian wars. We need to unite against challenges. We must be part of struggle we are waging against enemy. Our Ummah is facing difficulties. They are trying to make us forget cause." "Their struggle is enlightening whole world." Mehmet Güney, Honorary President of Human Civilization Movement, expressed support brothers resisting an independent free Palestine, stating, "Their struggle is enlightening whole world." He added, "I congratulate my brothers who are resisting an independent free Palestine. Their struggle is illuminating entire world. This congress is a step taken on path of Palestinian resistance freedom. I greet all of you who come to Istanbul from around world." "Role of Civil Society: A New Era in Struggle." On second day of symposium, focus will be on Islamic world's response to Aksa Deluge. conference will examine importance of civil society movements in Palestinian struggle, with evaluations on Zionism contributed by Sami Al-Arian Mohsen Saleh. final day of event will take place in Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, addressing "The Impact of Gaza Genocide on International Regional Orders." This topic will be discussed by Sami Al-Arian, Berdal Aral, François Burgat, Mr. Pepe Escobar, Mohammad Marandi, Joseph Massad, Firoz Osman.

title: IZU Students Condemn Israeli Oppression
content: IZU Students Condemn Israeli Oppression 09.10.2024 On anniversary of attacks initiated by Israel in Gaza last October, which have recently expanded to include strikes in Lebanon, protests are being held worldwide. Students from Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University gathered on Halkali campus to condemn Israeli oppression. The event, titled "From Resistance to Resurrection: Intifada Again," saw participation from various student clubs, including Ahdimiz Kudüs Platform, Emergency Aid Natural Environment Young Vision Good Deeds Law ISAR Islamic Economy Finance Islamic Studies Mehmet Akif Literature National Youth Pioneer Generation Guidance Youth Seven Crescent New Generation Student Club. The event was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Secretary General Dr. Hizir Tutan, as well as many department heads academics, who marched alongside students. At end of event, a press statement was issued, expressing that Israel's attacks have turned Palestine into a bloodbath. The statement highlighted that Israel targets everyone—young old, hospitals, schools, homes—without distinction, called for global public to unite to stop oppression. It emphasized that Western countries quickly sided with Israel stated that youth, especially on university campuses, must stand together as one voice against tyranny. "Acar: We Are Witnessing a Zionist Brutality Beyond Words." At event, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, who walked alongside students, remarked that one of most inhumane oppressions seen by humanity in recent times is occurring in Palestine, where a Zionist brutality continues that words fail to describe. He stated: “Israel has expanded its attacks towards Lebanon Syria. The 2 billion strong Islamic world, 52 Muslim countries, Organization of Turkish States, Arab League have unfortunately been unable to stop this oppression. We are witnessing how United Nations views Muslims with disdain. As some verses have foretold, we are learning that disbelief is a united force. But why is our situation like this? Because Allah commands unity, yet we choose division. As long as this separation continues, we will keep experiencing this humiliation. This is an imperialist war, a mixed war, a hybrid war, a proxy war using puppets. They exploit all kinds of differences to divide societies. Therefore, we must unite. For 200 years, Islamic world has lagged behind in race for science, industry, technology. Universities you, youth, have a significant responsibility here. We must unite, work hard, become ones who redefine civilization.”

title: IZU Students Walked for Women Victims of Violence
content: IZU Students Walked for Women Victims of Violence 09.10.2024 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University students gathered to raise awareness against violence towards women. Dressed in black, they carried banners placards, sending a message of unity. Addressing students, Vice Rector Prof. Ibrahim Güney stated that increasing instances of violence seen recently have no place in either our faith or our traditions. During walk, Prof. Güney joined students, who displayed photos of women victims of violence on their clothing. He emphasized that we have reached a point where words are no longer enough regarding violence against women, reiterating that such acts are unacceptable. Prof. Güney thanked students for their sensitivity commitment to this important cause. "WOMEN SHOULD FEEL SAFE" Speaking on behalf of students, Saide Seyma Akkol condemned all forms of violence, harassment, discrimination against women. Akkol expressed that it is everyone's responsibility to stand against violence, stating: "All segments of society should be part of solution against all forms of violence discrimination. Women should feel respect safety they deserve in all areas of society be able to live freely securely. With this aim, we express that we will continue to advocate for women's rights stand against all forms of violence. As emphasized in Farewell Sermon of Hz. Muhammad, women are entrusted to you by Allah. We see respecting women's dignity rights as a duty of humanity."

title: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence on Law Was Discussed
content: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence on Law Was Discussed 10.10.2024 Attorney Ali Göksu was guest at conference titled “From Legal Mind to Smart Law,” organized by Faculty of Law at IZÜ. During conference, which was also attended by our Vice Rector Prof. Ismail Küçük and Dean of Faculty of Law Prof. Mustafa Ates, Göksu discussed importance of artificial intelligence in legal field today and how this technology is transforming legal processes. A conference titled “From Legal Mind to Smart Law: Some Reflections on Redefining Role of Legal Knowledge in Information Society” was held at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. The program, organized by Faculty of Law at IZÜ in Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall, featured Attorney Ali Göksu as a guest. In addition to IZÜ Vice Rector Prof. Ismail Küçük and Dean of Faculty of Law Prof. Mustafa Ates, event was attended by academics and students. The opening speech was delivered by Dean Prof. Mustafa Ates, who stated that Göksu's experience in legal practices and his curiosity about technology would greatly benefit participants. Attorney Ali Göksu discussed importance of artificial intelligence in legal field today and how this technology is transforming legal processes. Highlighting changes brought about by digitization, Göksu emphasized importance of data management and effective use of information in legal practice. He stated, “Humanity has been in search of knowledge throughout history. Today, artificial intelligence can be considered a revolution in law. Technology has facilitated access to information and accelerated decision-making process.”

title: The Second Term of The "Children Moving with Fun" Project Has Started
content: The Second Term of The "Children Moving with Fun" Project Has Started 16.10.2024 The second term of the "Children Moving with Fun" project, conducted by the Faculty of Sports Sciences at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, has started. Under the coordination of Dr. Ibrahim Akgün, the project offers movement education for preschool students aged 3-6. The classes, where the little ones engage in sports while having fun, are taught by second-year students from the Faculty of Sports Sciences. The project aims to help faculty students gain teaching experience while enhancing children's motor skills, increasing body awareness, and contributing to their cognitive-emotional development.

title: About 2024-2025 Fall Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Schedule
content: About 2024-2025 Fall Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Schedule 21.10.2024 Students who are willing to apply for PhD qualifying exam to be held in fall semester of 2024-2025 academic year are required to fill out PhD Qualifying application form at https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/login between October 21-25, 2024 . PhD Qualifying Exams will be held on a date to be determined by relevant Head of Departments in December 2024 . The exams will be held at our Halkali campus. POINTS TO BE CONSIDERED 1- Students who have successfully completed their courses and seminar, have a cumulative grade point average of at least 3.00 and above, and fulfill Foreign Language Proficiency can apply for doctoral qualifying exam. 2- Students admitted with a master's degree must take doctoral qualifying exam until end of fifth semester at latest in compliance with relevant regulations. 3- In order for students to take PhD Qualifying Exam, they must have a thesis supervisor and choose from “PhD Qualifying Exam” section at https://derskayit.izu.edu.tr/Login 4- Students who applied for PhD Qualifying Examination held in spring semester of 2023-2024 academic year and did not take exam or failed exam, are required to apply again on specified dates. Students who do not apply will be considered unsuccessful and will be disenrolled from University. 5- Please click here for post-exam procedures of students who are successful in PhD qualifying exam.

title: A New Term Has Begun in the Mathematics Teaching Department.
content: A New Term Has Begun in the Mathematics Teaching Department. 25.09.2024 An orientation program was organized for new students of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University's Elementary Mathematics Teaching Department. An orientation program was held for new students of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University's Faculty of Educational Sciences, Elementary Mathematics Teaching Department, under the leadership of Associate Professor Dr. Elif Esra Arikan. The program provided students with detailed information to help them quickly adapt to university life, and also covered opportunities for double major programs, minor studies, and Erasmus exchanges.

title: Important Announcement for Students in the PhD Dissertation Proposal or Dissertation Monitoring Stage in the Fall Semester of 2024-2025
content: Important Announcement for Students in PhD Dissertation Proposal or Dissertation Monitoring Stage in Fall Semester of 2024-2025 30.09.2024 defenses/dissertation meetings for fall semester of 2024-2025 academic year must be held by end of office hours on Friday, December 31, 2024, at latest. Students who exceed this deadline will be considered unsuccessful in their proposals/dissertation stage. thesis documents of PhD students in fall semester must be submitted to Graduate Education Institute by Head of Department by Friday, January 3, 2025 , at latest. Last day for Dissertation Proposal Defenses/Dissertation Monitoring Meetings December 31, 2024 Last Day for Submission of Dissertation Proposal Defenses/ Dissertation Monitoring Documents January 3, 2025 POINTS TO CONSIDER According to IZU Graduate Education Training Regulations; Dissertation Proposal Defense ARTICLE 23 - (1) student, who successfully completes PhD qualifying exam, defends proposal, which includes purpose, method, study plan of research to be carried out, in front of committee, within six months at latest. delivers a written report on to committee members at least fifteen days before oral defense. (2) committee makes decision on acceptance, correction, or rejection of submitted by with an absolute majority. One month is given for correction. At end of this period, decision made in direction of acceptance or rejection by an absolute majority is notified to Institute by head of department, with a report within three days following end of process. (3) whose is rejected has right to choose a new supervisor and/or subject. In this case, a new committee able to be appointed. who wants to continue program with same supervisor is taken to defense again within three months, who changed supervisor subject within six months. whose is also rejected in this defense is disenrolled from University. time elapsed in these stages is counted from duration of student's PhD education. (5) who does not take defense within time specified in first paragraph without an acceptable excuse is considered unsuccessful, thesis is rejected. Dissertation Monitoring Meetings ARTICLE 23 - (4) For whose thesis is accepted, thesis committee meets at least twice a year, once in January-June once in July-December. submits a written report to committee members at least one month before meeting date. In this report, a summary of study done so far study plan for next semester are stated. student's thesis study is determined as successful or unsuccessful by committee. Students who are found unsuccessful by committee twice in a row or three times intermittently are dismissed from University. thesis report minutes must be submitted by thesis supervisors to Graduate Education Institute with approval of Head of Department through EBYS system. supervisors from outside IZU, must contact relevant Head of Department to submit documents of their students. PhD students who have successfully completed courses, seminars, at least three meetings within their deadlines, have a weighted grade point average of 3,00 above, meet publication requirement can take defense exam.

title: Important Announcement for PhD Students who are in the Thesis Period and whose Study Period Will Expire
content: Important Announcement Students are in Period whose Study Period Will Expire 30.09.2024 In programs of our Institute , supervisors of students, have completed standard study period of eight semesters or a maximum study period of twelve semesters (excluding Scientific Preparation, Suspension of Registration, Additional Time) by end of Fall Semester of 2024-2025 academic year, are required to submit Word version of to Control Team by end of office hours on Friday, December 13, 2024, at latest. Theses submitted after December 13, 2024, will not be accepted. defense forms of students will graduate in fall semester be submitted by Head of Department to Graduate Education Institute by Friday, December 27, 2024 . Students in this situation fulfill publication requirement before December 27, 2024. students will graduate in fall semester take defense by Friday, January 31, 2025. Last Submission Date Control December 13, 2024 Last Submission Date Defense Documents December 27, 2024 Deadline Fulfilling Publication Requirement December 27, 2024 Last Day of Defense Examinations January 31, 2025 POINTS TO CONSIDER student be successful in at least seven courses, a seminar course (60 ECTS), Qualifying (30 ECTS), Proposal (30 ECTS), (120 ECTS) periods (240 ECTS in total) have a CGPA of at least 3,00 to qualify to take dissertation exam. students enrolled in programs fulfil at least one of publication requirements. ( Click here information about publication requirements.) students, have not submitted their proposal to Graduate Education Institute through their supervisors on time, will not be able to take defense even if they have completed their thesis. Control of Plagiarism Writing Rules of of Student Whose is Completed primary responsibility preparation of in accordance Writing Guidelines belongs to student supervisor. Control Team members are responsible formal control of final drafts of theses. supervisor e-mail student's to Control Team members. Click here control e-mail addresses. Click here procedures that students supervisors follow during Control process. In Case of Maximum Period of Study Ends Failure students, are not able to complete their until mentioned day, within their normal study period of eight semesters extend their submission to Fall Semester of 2024-2025 academic year, are required to pay tuition one course extension period. (No discount will be applied to tuition fee paid extension period.) This situation is also valid other extension periods. students, have failed in study or have not been able to take defense within maximum study period of twelve semesters, will be disenrolled from University. According to IZU Graduate Education Teaching Regulation; Finalizing ARTICLE 24 - (1) A student in doctorate program writes results obtained in accordance institute writing guide defends his orally before jury. (2) Before defense of in theses given correction, student completes correction submits it to advisor. advisor presents plagiarism report to institute a written opinion that is defensible has been written in accordance institute writing guide. In case of a real plagiarism in data in report, is sent to institute board of directors to be decided together its justification. (3) In order student's to be finalized, three successful monitoring committee reports be submitted at least every six months. If monitoring is not done on time, student is deemed unsuccessful from monitoring result of that period. (4) jury is appointed recommendation of advisor head of department approval of institute board of directors. jury consists of five faculty members, including three lecturers on student's monitoring committee including advisor., at least two of them from outside University If advisor is from another higher education institution, jury consist of one faculty member outside university four faculty members inside university. institute board of directors decides whether consultant has right to vote. If advisor does not have right to vote, jury consists of six faculty members. In addition, second supervisor can take part in jury without right to vote. (5) members of jury gather at earliest fifteen days at latest one month from date is delivered to them, takes student to defense. defense consists of presentation of work question-answer section that follows. defense meetings are open to participation of lecturers, graduate students experts from field. (6) After completion of exam, jury of is closed to audience it decides to accept, reject or correct absolute majority. Students whose is accepted are evaluated as successful. This decision is notified to institute by department within three days following exam. student whose is unsuccessful rejected is dismissed from University. student, got a correction advice on his thesis, makes necessary corrections within six months at latest defends his before same jury. student fails in this defense is dismissed from University. Those have not been successful in but admitted to Ph.D an undergraduate degree, may get according to first paragraph of Article 16 a master diploma without thesis.

title: Important Announcement for Thesis Master's Students whose Normal-Maximum Study Period Will Expire
content: Important Announcement Master's Students whose Normal-Maximum Study Period Will Expire 01.10.2024 In Master's programs of our Institute, supervisors of students who have completed standard study period of four semesters or a maximum study period of six semesters (excluding Scientific Preparation, Registration Suspension, Additional Time) as of end of Fall Semester of 2024-2025 academic year are required to submit Word version of to Control Team by end of office hours on Friday, 13 December, 2024 , at latest. Theses submitted after December 13, 2024, will not be accepted. The forms defense of master's students graduating in Fall semester must be submitted by Head of Department to Graduate Education Institute by Friday, December 27, 2024 . The master students who will graduate in Fall semester must take defense exam by Friday , January 31, 2025 . Last Submission Date Control December 13, 2024 Last Submission Date Defense Documents December 27, 2024 Last Day of Defense Examinations January 31, 2025 Requirements Taking Defence Exam Students must have at least seven courses one Seminar course (a total of 60 ECTS) together with a minimum GPA of 2.50 to take defense exam. The title of determined by student together with their advisor must be listed in master's program. Checking Plagiarism Compliance with Writing Rules Completed Master's Theses The primary responsibility preparation of in accordance with Writing Guidelines belongs to student supervisor. Control Team members are responsible formal control of final drafts of theses. The supervisor must e-mail their student's to Control Team members. Click here control e-mail addresses. Click here procedures that students supervisors must follow during Control Unit. In Case of Period Extension Failure Students, who are not able to complete their until mentioned day, within their normal study period of four semesters extend their submission to Fall Semester of 2024-2025 academic year, are required to pay tuition one course extension period. (No discount will be applied to tuition fee paid extension period.) This situation is also valid second extension period. Students, who have failed in study or have not been able to take defense exam within maximum study period of six semesters, will be dismissed from University. According to 1st 2nd paragraphs of Article 9 of IZU Graduate Education Training Regulations; (1) The duration of master's program with is four semesters, starting from semester in which courses related to program enrolled in program are given, regardless of whether they are registered each semester, except period spent in scientific preparation, program is completed in a maximum of six semesters. (2) The student who has failed to complete credit courses one Seminar course or has not been able to fulfill success criteria determined by University, who has failed in study or has not been able to take defense exam within maximum period of study will be disenrolled from University.

title: Important Announcement for Non-Thesis Master's Students of the 2024-2025 Fall Semester
content: Important Announcement Non-Thesis Master's Students of 2024-2025 Fall Semester 01.10.2024 In non-thesis master's programs of our Institute supervisors of students who have completed their study period by end of 2024-2025 academic year's fall semester are required to submit Word version of to control email addresses by end of office hours on Friday, December 20, 2024 , at latest. Projects submitted after this date will not be accepted. Plagiarism reports and plagiarism declaration forms of non-thesis master's students graduating in fall semester must be submitted by Head of Department to Graduate Education Institute by Friday, January 17, 2025 , at latest. Non-thesis master's students graduating in fall semester must submit their projects by Friday, January 17, 2025 . Last Submission Date Control December 20, 2024 Last Submission Date Documents January 17, 2025 Deadline Submission January 17, 2025 Requirements Submission of a Non Thesis The students enrolled in non-thesis Master's programs must have at least ten courses (a total of 75 ECTS) together with a minimum GPA of 2.50 to submit. For a non-thesis master's program, title determined by student and their supervisor must be listed in . Checking Plagiarism and Compliance with Writing Rules Completed Master's The primary responsibility preparation of in accordance with Graduate Education Institute Thesis Writing Guide belongs to student and supervisor. The supervisor should send student's via email to relevant Control Team. Click here email addresses of Control Team. Click here procedures students and supervisors must follow during Control process. In case of Incompletion and Failure Students who fail to complete their master’s or submit their projects within three-semester study period will be dismissed from University. According to 1st paragraph of article 14 of IZU Graduate Education and Training Regulations; (1) The duration of master's program with non-thesis is at least two semesters starting from semester in which courses related to program enrolled in program are given, regardless of whether they are registered each semester, except period spent in scientific preparation, and program is completed in at most three semesters,

title: Our Graduate Student Congress has started
content: Our Graduate Student Congress has started 02.10.2024 The "Graduate Student Congress," organized in collaboration with Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University and Ilim Yayma Foundation, has begun at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. After opening remarks by our Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, and Ilim Yayma Foundation General Director Idris Tankut Küsmezer, our guest, Prof. Dr. Zekeriya Kursun, Vice Rector of FSM Foundation University, delivered a speech titled "Some Assessments on Methodological Issues in Middle Eastern Studies." In his opening speech at congress, Vice Rector of IZÜ, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, emphasized opportunities presented to participants. He noted that organizing congress in collaboration with Ilim Yayma Foundation offers significant opportunities for academic careers, stating, “Graduate congresses provide students chance to present their independent work and expand their academic networks. The number of participants is also quite satisfactory.” "We are on path to raising good people." Ilim Yayma Foundation General Director Idris Tankut Küsmezer stated, "Our society, established in 1951, and our foundation, founded in 1973, are dedicated to raising good people. We have carried out activities on this path. We are delighted to meet with our graduate students, who have potential to become scientists of future, under university's roof." "In a scientific environment, Western perspectives are dominant." IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized challenges of conducting research, especially in social sciences. He continued his speech by stating, "We see that Western perspectives dominate scientific environment in Turkey and other Islamic countries. If we, as Turkey, operate solely through viewpoints of American and Western researchers, our risk of reaching similar conclusions increases. Incentives for publications in foreign indexes can leave manipulative traces on scientists. I believe these issues need to be addressed. It is important for graduate students to take responsibility in this matter." "Correctly defining problems is first step in finding solutions." After protocol addresses, opening conference commenced. Prof. Dr. Zekeriya Kursun, Vice Rector of FSM Foundation University, delivered a speech titled "Some Assessments on Methodological Issues in Middle Eastern Studies." He addressed methodological challenges faced in Middle Eastern studies and emphasized importance of an interdisciplinary approach to resolving these issues. Kursun noted that correctly defining problems is first step in finding solutions to chronic issues in region.

title: TÜBITAK Provides Project Support to Kurnaz
content: TÜBITAK Provides Project Support to Kurnaz 02.10.2024 The 2024 First Term Application Results of the 2218 Domestic Post-Doctoral Research Scholarship Program, conducted by TÜBITAK's Science Human Support Programs Directorate, have been announced. Dr. Ahmet Kurnaz from the Architecture Department of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University has been awarded support for his project by TÜBITAK. As part of the TÜBITAK 2218 Domestic Post-Doctoral Research Scholarship Program, Dr. Ahmet Kurnaz from the Architecture Department of the Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, under the supervision of Prof. Dr. Çigdem Polatoglu Serter from Yildiz Technical University, has had his project titled "The Preservation of Agricultural Industrial Structures within the Context of Industrial Heritage and Their Tourism-Oriented Reuse: A Study on Traditional Olive Oil Factories in the Edremit Gulf" recognized for support by TÜBITAK.

title: Entrepreneurs Meet with IZÜ Students
content: Entrepreneurs Meet with IZÜ Students 02.10.2024 The conference titled "The Future of Entrepreneurship, E-Commerce, Digital Marketing," organized by our E-Commerce Club, has begun. In opening remarks, IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized changing dynamics of global trade importance of digital literacy, as many entrepreneurs gathered with our students for event. The conference titled "The Future of Entrepreneurship, E-Commerce, Digital Marketing," organized by E-Commerce Club under Health, Culture, Sports Department of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, was held at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The opening speech was given by Emre Koca, founder of MentorX, who shared his entrepreneurial journey story behind MentorX, highlighting innovations that digitalization brings to business world. Koca stated, "The opportunities offered in e-commerce present a significant chance for students. We see how marketing stands out. The roles of different actors in this process are crucial. I believe we will achieve successful projects together." REKTOR ACAR: INCREASE YOUR DIGITAL LITERACY. In his opening speech, IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar discussed globalization e-commerce. He noted that with acceleration of digitalization, entrepreneurs need to develop new business models, stating, "We are in an era where history is rapidly unfolding. Economic activities are conducted on a global scale. Communication transportation have expanded beyond national borders. Smart technologies have swiftly entered our lives. Transportation has become widespread, efficient, fast. As capital accumulation increases, national markets become insufficient. If more functional products can be obtained at better prices abroad, people chase after them. This brings both opportunities threats. Supply chain management will be addressed more comprehensively, marketing e-commerce applications have gained significant importance in today’s competitive global world. Israel's recent use of smartphones in Lebanon is latest example of threats in digital world. We are experiencing a radical transformation together. Artificial intelligence is now writing for us, even coding. E-commerce offers immense opportunities for young people to gain their first entrepreneurial experiences. My advice to youth is to enhance your experiences in digital literacy." Entrepreneurs Shared Their Inspiring Stories. Following Rector Acar's speech, program moved on to its first panel. Moderated by Tuger Akkaya, panel featured Inanç Ayar, Ömer Seyfettin Sener, Isa Korkmaz, who shared their experiences on "Entrepreneurships Shaping Future." The three entrepreneurs provided inspiring examples of how digital transformation new technologies offer strategic opportunities in business world. In second session, moderated by Emre Koca, Furkan Talay, Sedat Akdemir, Batuhan Kizil discussed "Comparing Organic Traffic Paid Traffic in E-Export." The panelists shared insights with participants on how traffic management in e-commerce digital marketing can be effectively developed through right strategies.

title: In the Orientation Program for Future Teachers
content: In the Orientation Program for Future Teachers 02.10.2024 A comprehensive orientation program was held for new students in the Basic Education Department at IZÜ. Led by Prof. Dr. Hatice Vatansever Bayraktar, the Head of the Basic Education Department, the program included participation from Dr. Ögr. Üyesi Ogün Çakir, Head of the Classroom Education Department, and other faculty members. The program provided detailed information to help students quickly adapt to university life, covering topics such as Double Major (ÇAP), minor programs, and Erasmus opportunities. Additionally, discussions included career opportunities after graduation, teaching practices, and internship options.

title: The Opening Ceremony Of The 14th Academic Year Of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Was Successfully Held.
content: The Opening Ceremony Of The 14th Academic Year Of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Was Successfully Held. 25.09.2024 The opening ceremony of 14th academic year at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University was held in museum hall. The event was attended by university's president, Professor Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, along with vice presidents Professor Dr. Ibrahim Günes, Professor Dr. Ismail Küçük, Professor Dr. Erhan Içener, Secretary General Dr. Hizir Tutan, as well as members of academic administrative staff. The event also saw a significant attendance from students who were taking their first steps into university life. The ceremony began with Turkish national anthem a moment of silence, followed by screening of a university promotional video. In his opening speech, President Professor Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar expressed his hope that new academic year would be prosperous for students, faculty, administrative staff. "Ilim Yayma" is an institution committed to values of our civilization. In his speech, Professor Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized that Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University is a foundation university established in 2010 by Ilim Yayma Foundation, stressing foundation's commitment to values of civilization. Acar said: “For many years, Ilim Yayma Foundation has been supporting national spiritual values by providing scholarships accommodation to thousands of students in order to support educational efforts. Inspired by this spirit, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University offers, with its diverse faculties, departments, international students, library, sports facilities, laboratories, a campus environment that is both at heart of city surrounded by nature. The university is named after Professor Dr. Sabahattin Zaim, who was a pioneer in Islamic economics participation banking. The university continues its educational mission on a campus that has been providing education since 1892 at historic Halkali School of Agriculture, where our Independence Poet Mehmet Akif Ersoy both studied taught. Our university draws inspiration from these two distinguished figures Ilim Yayma Foundation. On this special day, we welcome all our new students wish each of you a peaceful successful academic life."

title: Bahçecik: IZÜ Will Illuminate Your Education
content: Bahçecik: IZÜ Will Illuminate Your Education 25.09.2024 The Faculty of Health Sciences held its orientation program at Nizam al-Mulk Hall. In her speech, Dean Bahçecik stated that IZÜ will illuminate education of its students. The Faculty of Health Sciences held its orientation program for 2024-2025 academic year at Nizam al-Mulk Hall. The event was attended by IZÜ Health Sciences Faculty Dean, Prof. Dr. Ayse Nefise Bahçecik, faculty board members, academic and administrative staff, as well as students embarking on their university journey in field of health sciences. In her speech, Prof. Bahçecik wished for health, success, and happiness in new academic term, highlighting importance of preparing students for future. She emphasized that orientation process is a valuable opportunity and encouraged students to embrace it as a cherished memory. "A University That Illuminates Your Education in Every Aspect" Reflecting on university’s rich history, Bahçecik continued: “We have a human-centered work plan. Our faculty has nearly 850 students, with around 60 international students. Particularly in international activities, a wide range of opportunities is offered to students. Participating in Erasmus programs will open new doors in your life. Over next four years, you will collaborate with your esteemed professors. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, with its campus, facilities, academic staff, and diverse range of activities, will illuminate your education in every aspect.”

title: Is Artificial Intelligence a Friend or Foe?
content: Is Artificial Intelligence a Friend or Foe? 25.09.2024 In opening lecture of Faculty of Business and Management Sciences, role and risks of artificial intelligence in education were evaluated. The opening lecture for 2024-2025 academic year at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University’s Faculty of Business and Management Sciences was delivered by Prof. Dr. Veysel Bozkurt, a faculty member from Istanbul University. Titled "The Use of Artificial Intelligence in Education: Opportunities and Risks," program was held in ISEFAM Abdullah Tivnikli Hall. The event thoroughly examined role of artificial intelligence in education, opportunities it presents, and risks it entails. Prof. Yumusak: Unlimited Use Poses Risks In his opening speech, Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güran Yumusak, Dean of Faculty of Business and Management Sciences at IZÜ, evaluated impacts of artificial intelligence on our lives. He stated, "The risks brought about by unlimited usage must be taken seriously." Prof. Yumusak emphasized, "Artificial intelligence is a new field. Alongside its advantages, it carries significant risks. During pandemic, we focused heavily on online classes; however, now we need to discuss consequences of situation. Considering cellphone bans in schools in Germany, we must prevent negative effects of technology on young people." Prof. Bozkurt: Could Artificial Intelligence Bring About End of Humanity? In opening lecture, Prof. Dr. Veysel Bozkurt, a faculty member at Istanbul University, discussed impacts of artificial intelligence on our lives and its potential for future. He noted AI is one of most variable and transformative concepts, highlighting number of people actively using AI technology is increasing daily. Prof. Bozkurt also mentioned there is a significant population perceives AI as a threat, stating, "In a study conducted with a group of 1,500 people, 20% expressed AI would bring about end of humanity. According to analyses, AI is seen as a threat by many. In-depth analyses of this technology are being conducted. AI is with us at every stage of our lives. Whether you use it or not, AI is a part of your life. There is a prevailing perception AI will increase unemployment. So, can AI really bring about end of humanity? I wish we could answer question. How AI will impact our lives depends on technology companies. We need to produce technology belongs to us. This is where our fundamental problem lies. AI is something neither you nor I can afford to give up."

title: Guidance Teachers Gathered at IZÜ
content: Teachers Gathered at IZÜ 25.09.2024 teachers working in Küçükçekmece region gathered at IZÜ to set their strategies for 2024-2025 academic year. The meeting discussed various issues aimed at enhancing quality of guidance services, including prevention of violence in schools. Hosted by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZÜ), "2024-2025 Academic Year Teachers Meeting" was organized in collaboration with Küçükçekmece District National Education Directorate, Küçükçekmece Research Center, IZÜ Corporate Communication Promotion Department. Approximately 250 guidance teachers from Küçükçekmece district participated in event, which began with National Anthem a moment of silence. The meeting was also attended by IZÜ Vice Rector Dean of Faculty of Educational Sciences, Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Küçükçekmece Special Education Branch Manager Pasali Besli, Küçükçekmece Research Center Director Tuncay Sabak. GÜNEY: GUIDANCE SERVICES ARE VERY IMPORTANT IN EDUCATION Expressing his pleasure in hosting event, IZÜ Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney emphasized significance of guidance in education. Güney stated, "The services provided by Research Center (RAM) are extremely important. You all carry out this service with great dedication. I congratulate each every one of you. We take pride in collaborating on projects with our faculty members, administrators, other institutions working in field of guidance supporting them. We always support such activities." PREVENTION OF VIOLENCE IN SCHOOLS WAS ALSO DISCUSSED Küçükçekmece Special Education Branch Director Pasali Besli Küçükçekmece Research Center Director Tuncay Sabak expressed their gratitude to Prof. Dr. Güney in their speeches. During program, guidance special education activities for 2023-2024 academic year were evaluated. Presentations continued with information about activities to be carried out by Research Center (RAM) in 2024-2025 academic year. The agenda of program included topics such as reviewing counseling programs, planned activities for prevention of violence in schools, psychosocial protection crisis intervention services, evaluation of special education activities.

title: 6th Alumni Meeting Held
content: 6th Alumni Meeting Held 25.09.2024 The 6th Alumni Meeting of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University took place in inner courtyard. The event was attended by our Rector, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, as IZÜ alumni reminisced about their past days. The 6th Alumni Reunion organized by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Alumni and Members Association (IZÜMED) took place in historic inner courtyard of campus. The event was attended by our Rector, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, as well as Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney and Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener. IZÜ alumni showed great interest in event, which was further enriched by participation of graduates' spouses and children, creating colorful moments. Activities included play areas, delicious refreshments, sports activities, music performances, and workshops. Rector Acar visited each stand to chat with alumni and listen to their memories of university life. One of highlights of event was entertainment, particularly enjoyed by children. The program continued with a music performance, providing participants with unforgettable moments. During event, IZÜ alumni had opportunity to revisit their old school with their families, refreshing their past memories.

title: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Mohammed Wadi Entered the Top 2% of the SCOPUS List
content: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Mohammed Wadi Entered Top 2% of SCOPUS List 25.09.2024 Our Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences member, Assoc. Prof. Dr. Mohammed Wadi, has succeeded in ranking in top 2% in field of Electrical and Electronics Engineering on SCOPUS list. Assoc. Prof. Dr. Mohammed Wadi from Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University’s Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences has been ranked in top 2% in field of Electrical and Electronics Engineering according to "Updated Science-Wide Author Databases of Standardized Citation Indicators" study based on SCOPUS citations. The list evaluates 100,000 scientists from around world and is constructed using a composite indicator (C-SCORE) that includes fundamental factors such as H-index and co-authorship adjusted HM-index, which are of significant academic importance. Expressing his thoughts on this achievement, Assoc. Prof. Dr. Mohammed Wadi stated that he is very happy to announce this important success, and that international recognition of his research serves as a significant motivation for him. Wadi also extended his gratitude to Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University for inspiring him throughout his education, development, and research journey.

title: A Mevlid-i Nebi program was organized at IZÜ.
content: A Mevlid-i Nebi program was organized at IZÜ. 25.09.2024 A meaningful program was held at IZÜ as part of Mawlid al-Nabi Week, marking 1,453rd anniversary of birth of Prophet Muhammad (pbuh). The event took place in Abdullah Tivnkli Conference Hall at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, attended by our Rector, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, as well as a significant number of academic administrative staff students. The program began with recitation of Quran by Hafiz Necip Fazil Belge, an Arabic Preparatory student winner of Quran recitation competition. In his speech, Rector Prof. Dr. Acar emphasized importance of Mawlid tradition in Islamic culture. He highlighted its sociological value, stating: “The Mawlid is an important text that conveys fundamental tenets of Islam exemplary life of Prophet Muhammad to broad audiences. It is particularly valuable in passing on these teachings to younger generations enriching their spiritual lives. Such programs will not only enhance our spiritual world but also strengthen our unity solidarity in face of challenges.” "THE MAWLID AL-NABI IS A REFLECTION OF LOVE AND FAITH" The Dean of Faculty of Islamic Sciences, Prof. Dr. Hasan Kâmil Yilmaz, addressed significance historical context of Mawlid al-Nabi in his talk titled "The 5 Ws 1 H of Mawlid al-Nabi." He emphasized importance of Prophet Muhammad's (pbuh) birthday in Islamic world shared insights about historical origins of programs held on this special day. Yilmaz stated, "Mawlid al-Nabi is a reflection of profound love faith that we hold for life teachings of Prophet Muhammad (pbuh)." MEHMET ÖNCEL AND HIS TEAM PERFORMED A MUSIC RECITAL The program featured a music recital consisting of pieces in form of hymns, performed by Assoc. Prof. Dr. Mehmet Öncel, head of Music Education Department of Fine Arts Education, his team. The event concluded with a prayer by Dr. Adem Ergül. At end of event, a raffle was held where five participants received gift vouchers for use with Turkish Religious Foundation Publications.

title: Academic Writing Training at IZÜ
content: Academic Writing Training at IZÜ 23.08.2024 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University hosted a significant training session on academic writing, conducted by Associate Professor Dr. Mustafa Özgenel as part of the Erasmus+ Core Project. In the morning session, Dr. Özgenel addressed common mistakes encountered in the academic writing process. He emphasized the importance of not only writing a quality article but also choosing the right journal and understanding the submission process. Dr. Özgenel highlighted the critical role of peer review and ethical considerations in scientific processes, offering valuable tips based on his own experiences. The afternoon session featured a workshop led by Associate Professor Dr. Osman Özdemir from the Turkish Education Department at IZÜ. The workshop involved various activities using brainstorming techniques to create different sections of academic papers.

title: Significant Meeting at IZÜ’s Center for Islam and Global Affairs
content: Significant Meeting at IZÜ’s Center for Islam and Global Affairs 06.09.2024 Prof. Dr. Sami Al-Arian, Director of the Center for Islam and Global Affairs (CIGA) at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, met with Serkan Kayalar, President of the Turkish Cooperation and Coordination Agency. The visit aimed to strengthen cooperation between the two institutions and to exchange ideas, particularly on current issues in the Islamic world. During the meeting, discussions also focused on regional challenges and potential collaborative projects aimed at addressing these issues. This meeting is considered an important step towards enhancing academic and humanitarian partnerships.

title: Uzbekistan Nordic International University and IZÜ Sign MOU for Academic Cooperation
content: Uzbekistan Nordic International University IZÜ Sign MOU for Academic Cooperation 02.09.2024 In an exciting development, Uzbekistan Nordic International University Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University have officially signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) aimed at fostering academic cooperation exchange between the two institutions. The Uzbekistan delegation, led by Vice-Rector of Scientific Affairs Innovations, Dr. Odil Kuysinov, Head of the Department of International Relations, Abdurauf Tolibov, met with their counterparts from Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. The Turkish university was represented by Vice Rector Dean of Engineering Faculty, Prof. Ismail KÜÇÜK, Vice Rector Dean of Education Faculty, Prof. Ibrahim Güney, the Head of the International Office team, Ilhan Yalim. This newly signed MOU establishes a solid foundation for future collaborations, including joint educational programs, research projects, student faculty exchange initiatives. Both universities are eagerly anticipating the new opportunities mutual benefits that this partnership will bring.

title: IZU Became a Finalist at TEKNOFEST!
content: IZU Became a Finalist at TEKNOFEST! 11.09.2024 Turkey’s largest space, aviation, and technology festival, TEKNOFEST, concluded its Technology Competitions in Antalya with great enthusiasm. The closing ceremony of Technology Competitions, held as part of TEKNOFEST Mediterranean, was attended by Selçuk Bayraktar, Chairman of TEKNOFEST Board, Prof. Dr. Haluk Görgün, President of Defense Industry, and Hulusi Sahin, Governor of Antalya. Thousands of technology enthusiasts brought Mediterranean to life with technology over course of four days through their innovative projects and exciting competitions. Participants from various age groups and skill levels, ranging from young engineering candidates to entrepreneurs, showcased projects that will shape technologies of future. Teams engaged in fierce competition throughout contests, displaying their technological expertise and creativity to fullest. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University distinguished itself at TEKNOFEST, Turkey's largest technology festival, by making it to finals in Barrier-Free Living Technologies category out of 76,000 applications. The STARSEED team achieved this significant success with their Dixlearning application, developed for students with specific learning difficulties.

title: IZU Faculty of Education Earns the Right to Use TYÇ Logo!
content: IZU Faculty of Education Earns Right to Use TYÇ Logo! 27.07.2024 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University's Faculty of Education has earned right to use Turkey Qualifications Framework (TYÇ) logo on diplomas of its Arabic Language Teaching, English Language Teaching, Special Education Teaching, Primary School Teaching, Turkish Language Teaching undergraduate programs. Starting from 2023-2024 Academic Year, TYÇ logo, which signifies high standards quality in education employment both domestically internationally, will be featured on diploma supplements, graduation certificates, transcripts of graduates from these programs. What is TYÇ? The Turkey Qualifications Framework (TYÇ) is a national qualifications framework designed to be compatible with European Qualifications Framework (EQF). It outlines principles for all qualifications acquired through formal, non-formal, informal learning, including vocational, general, academic education training programs from primary, secondary, higher education. TYÇ provides an integrated structure for classifying qualifications in Turkey, ensuring quality assurance across all educational levels learning pathways, including primary, secondary, higher education. Designed in alignment with EQF, TYÇ classifies all qualifications acquired through various learning paths into eight levels. These levels are based on complexity difficulty of qualifications, with Level 1 being simplest Level 8 most complex. Each level is defined by minimum common learning outcomes in terms of knowledge, skills, competencies, known as "level descriptors," which form core elements of TYÇ's design. The level descriptors provide foundation for other structures tools within TYÇ, ensuring a cohesive comprehensive framework for all qualifications in Turkey. For more information about Turkey Qualifications Framework (TYÇ), please visit: https://www.tyc.gov.tr/

title: IZU at the International Student Summit!
content: IZU at International Student Summit! 27.07.2024 The International Student Summit (ISTSUM), organized by Bab-i Alem International Student Association, has commenced in Istanbul. Held at Fatih Neslisah Sultan Cultural Center under theme "Climate and Environment," summit attracted students and academicians from various countries. Speakers emphasized significance of education and awareness in addressing climate and environmental issues, and also highlighted need to consider right to life. Discussions also covered Turkey's success in attracting international students and importance of environmental projects. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU) participated in ISTSUM, with its booth receiving significant attention. Former Ambassador Prof. Dr. Ahmet Kavas visited IZU booth and learned about university's international activities. Kavas remarked that being an international student requires great dedication and sacrifice, noting that out of 230 million students studying at universities worldwide, around 8 million are international students.
