The event was held as part of the annual tree planting campaign organized each November by the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry, and was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, and Prof. Dr. Nihat Erdogmus, along with IZU Erasmus Coordinator Mehmet Fatih Metehanoglu and Erasmus students.

title: Scientific Approaches to the Origins of Language Discussed
content: Scientific Approaches to the Origins of Language Discussed 24.11.2025 The program titled “Scientific Pursuits in Addressing the Problem of the Origin of Language,” organized by the Department of Turkish Language Teaching, was held in Seminar Hall 2. Prof. Dr. Caner Kerimoglu from Dokuz Eylül University attended as the keynote speaker and presented current scientific approaches to the emergence of language. Kerimoglu emphasized that the origins of language cannot be explained solely through written sources and noted that disciplines such as biology, archaeology, and neuroscience also contribute significantly to this field of research. The conference concluded with a question and answer session attended by students and academics.

title: An Orientation Program Held for Future Food Engineers
content: An Orientation Program Held for Future Food Engineers 26.11.2025 The “Preparing Future Engineers for Sector Orientation Program,” organized in cooperation with Department of Food Engineering, Food Agriculture Research Application Center, Food Engineering Club, was held at IZU. The program, which addressed current practices in food industry, laboratory work, professional development, began with a qanun performance. In opening session, Assoc. Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Gülseren Assoc. Prof. Dr. Ertan Ermis, faculty members of Department of Food Engineering, provided information about department. Dr. Zahir Far from University of Jijel in Algeria offered international insights into field. Introducing projects conducted at Biotechnology, Environment, Health Research Laboratory, Dr. Far also informed students about research opportunities academic collaborations. Sebahat Sisman, a Senior Food Engineer at A&T Food Control Laboratory, delivered a comprehensive presentation on food safety, laboratory analysis procedures, innovative next-generation quality control methods emerging in sector. The program concluded with various competitions social activities.

title: IZU hosted an important Meeting Within the Scope of University-Industry Collaboration
content: IZU hosted an important Meeting Within Scope of University-Industry Collaboration 26.11.2025 At breakfast program attended by MÜSAID delegation including MÜSAID President Burhan Özdemir and board members following participants were present from IZU: Chairman of Board of Trustees Özkan Göksal, Board Members Bekir Sakib Sahinoglu and Idris Turan Ilter, Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener and Prof. Dr. Nihat Erdogmus, IZU Secretary General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir, and Ilim Yayma Foundation General Manager Idris Tankut Küsmezer. During program, importance of strengthening collaboration between business world and academia was emphasized.

title: Architectural Traces of Hürrem Sultan
content: Architectural Traces of Hürrem Sultan 26.11.2025 The seminar "Haseki Hürrem Sultan's Architectural Works," organized by the Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Department of Architecture, was held in Seminar Hall 1 of the Historical Building. In the program, where Senior Architect Sehri Kartal was the guest speaker, the structures commissioned by Hürrem Sultan wife of Sultan Suleiman the Magnificent and mother of second Sultan Selim were discussed in terms of their historical context and architectural characteristics.

title: The issue of Ethics in Engineering
content: The issue of Ethics in Engineering 26.11.2025 The Engineering and Ethics Seminar organized by IZU Department of Industrial Engineering was held in Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. In program, where Dr. Üzeyir Pala, faculty member of Industrial Engineering Department, served as speaker, topics such as origins of engineering and ethical concepts, framework and importance of professional ethics, points where science and philosophy intersect or diverge, and consequences of unethical professional practices were discussed. In his talk, Pala explored questions such as: "Is everything legal necessarily ethical?", "Who is an engineer?", "What is engineering ?", "What are ethics, morality, and virtue, and how do they function at different stages?"

title: Companies at Zaim Technopark Met with IZU Stidents
content: Companies at Zaim Technopark Met with IZU Stidents 26.11.2025 Companies operating within IZU Zaim Technopark met with IZU students at the Career Fair. Held in the foyar of the Central Classroom Building, the event aimed to create direct interaction between students and industry representatives and drew significant participation. In addition to students, the fair was visited by IZU Chairman of the Board of Trustees Özkan Göksal, Board Member Idris Turan Ilter, Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, and Prof. Dr. Nihat Erdogmus, IZU Secretary General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir, MÜSAID President Burhan Özdemir, Ilim Yayma Foundation General Manager Idris Tankut Küsmezer, Technopark Director Dr. Özgür Özdemir, and various business representatives.

title: A "Silent Cry" Against Violence Toward Women
content: A "Silent Cry" Against Violence Toward Women 26.11.2025 The "Silent Cry" exhibition, organized by the IZU Department of Architecture, opened for visitors in the Museum Hall of the Historical Building. Prepared by Architecture faculty members Dr. Mahsa Hakki, Dr. Aysegül Gemci, Dr. Hüseyin Zülfikar, and assistant designers, the exhibition aims to draw attention to violence against women. Featuring 71 works created using 3D technology based on real life stories, the exhibition allows visitors to observe different dimensions of violence through interactive experiences. Open until the evening of Wednesday, November 26, the exhibition seeks to raise societal awareness of violence against women.

title: Teachers’ Day Summit
content: Teachers’ Day Summit 26.11.2025 The “November 24 Teachers’ Day Summit”, organized in collaboration with the IZU Department of Classroom Teaching and the Classroom Teaching Club, was held in the Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. Moderated by Dr. Ayse Isik Öner, the event featured Prof. Dr. Hatice Vatansever Bayraktar, Assoc. Dr. Elif Esra Arikan, Dr. Ogün Çakir, and Dr. Hümeyra Uysal as panelists. Following the panel, graduation teachers from the department shared Teachers’ Day celebration videos they had prepared together with their students.

title: IZU Cinema Club Hosted Director Mesut Uçakan
content: IZU Cinema Club Hosted Director Mesut 05.11.2025 Celebrated film director Mesut met with Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University students. Following a screening of film Reis Bey , engaged in a talk session, answering students' question about film cinematic career. Known for memorable films from 1990s suchj as Reis bey , Kelebekler Sonsuza Uçar , Yalniz Degilsiniz, Ölümsüz Karanfiller, Mesut was hosted by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. The Program titled "Behind Screen: Reis Bey Mesut Uçakan," organized by IZU Cinema Club, took place at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. Participants had opportunity to watch 1988 film Reis Bey, adapted from Necip Fazil Kisakürek's theatrical work of same name, later listen to a talk by film's director. "THE SUCCESS OF REIS BEY STEMS FROM THE DEPTH NECIP FAZIL BROUGHT TO THE STORY" Speaking at event, which was attended by IZU Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Nihat Erdogmus along with academic administrative staff students, responded to question about Reis Bey cinematic journey. Explaining film continues to attract interest despite yerars have passed, attributed its success to profound depth Necip Fazil brought to narrative. Reis Bey is work in which Master most clearly expresses Islam’s saving role,” he said, adding all of Necip Fazil’s works serve this vision. “I HAVE BEEN INTERESTED IN LITERATURE AND CINEMA SINCE SECONDARY SCHOOL” Sharing interest in literature cinema began during secondary school years, noted he dreamed of making Reis Bey even at time. He continued, “The Master’s life story is story of Turkey’s cultural transformation. Therefore, adapting works to cinema was a significant necessity. We completed script for Reis Bey with minimal alterations, making only small revisions to its powerful narrative, then filmed it.” “I FACED FINANCIAL CHALLENGES AT EVERY STAGE OF MY FILMS” Recounting move to Istanbul for university, where he first became involved in journalism later cinema, emphasized influence of National Turkish Students Union (MTTB) Cinema Club on career. Describing those years as a period of “scarcity,” he stated he attempted to make films with “ideological consciousness” in an industry he believed was dominated by Hollywood influence. Sharing he addressed politically sensitive subjects such as headscarf bans, unsolved political murders, Hat Reform, he openly embraced a religious identity, explained he faced severe financial difficulties at every stage of film production could not fully realize cinematic vision due to budget constraints.

title: The 2019 Ilim Yayma Awards Winners Met with Doctoral Students
content: The 2019 Ilim Yayma Awards Winners Met with Doctoral Students 03.11.2025 The scholars who received Ilim Yayma Awards in 2019 met with doctoral students at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU). Over a two-day program, academics shared their experiences insights with their young colleagues. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University hosted event titled “Experience Speaks,” which brought together recipients of Ilim Yayma Awards doctoral students. The program, held in IZU Museum Hall, was attended by IZU Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar, Chairman of Ilim Yayma Awards Commission Idris Sarisoy, Member of Ilim Yayma Awards Academic Commission Assoc. Rahman Dag. During event, Fikrettin Sahin, recipient of 2019 Grand Ilim Yayma Award, Mehmet Zahmakiran, winner of Engineering, Natural, Health Sciences Award, Ilyas Kemaloglu, winner of Social Sciences Award, shared their academic experiences perspectives with doctoral students from various universities. SAHIN: “True Independence Requires Reducing External Dependence” Speaking at a session moderated by IZU Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar, Fikrettin Sahin emphasized that for a country to be truly independent, it must reduce its reliance on external sources. “We aim to achieve new developments by investing in biotechnology. In this regard, scientists bear significant responsibilities,” he said. ZAHMAKIRAN: “The Key to Success Is Curiosity a Desire for Lifelong Learning” Mehmet Zahmakiran highlighted that curiosity a continuous desire to learn are at heart of being a successful scientist. He stated: “If you want to become a good scientist, you must constantly ask yourself ‘why?’. Set realistic goals. When improving your foreign language skills, don’t just study to pass exams-learn to truly understand.” KEMALOGLU: “The Perception of History Has Changed” Ilyas Kemaloglu drew attention to digital transformation in field of history. “The perception of history has changed thanks to academic research. Digital history studies have taken historical research to an entirely new level,” he said, emphasizing importance of language in historical studies. He noted that research meticulously examining local elements reveals valuable new insights about culture geography of regions studied. “Lantern Night Tour” at Yedikule Dungeons On second day of program, participants visited Sultanahmet Mosque, Hagia Sophia Grand Mosque, Gülhane Park, Spice Bazaar, accompanied by award-winning academics. The gathering concluded with a “Lantern Night Tour” held at Yedikule Dungeons.

title: Strategic Cooperation Protocol Signed Between IZU and the Turkish Maarif Foundation
content: Strategic Cooperation Protocol Signed Between IZU and Turkish Maarif Foundation 17.11.2025 A comprehensive cooperation protocol was signed between Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University and Turkish Maarif Foundation with aim of strengthening mutual collaboration and enhancing academic solidarity in field of education. The signing ceremony was attended by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar and Turkish Maarif Foundation President Mahmut Özdil . IZU Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük and International Students Coordinator Nesibe Aloudatallah Ghadban were also present at ceremony. The protocol represents a strategic step toward advancing shared goal of both institutions to contribute to high-quality education on a global scale. Within scope of agreement, more than 70,000 students in 56 countries where Turkish Maarif Foundation operates will be provided with scholarship opportunities and avenues for academic development. In addition, protocol aims to foster sustainable cooperation between two institutions by developing educational programs aligned with IZU’s academic infrastructure and international vision, guiding students toward higher education opportunities in Türkiye, and enhancing joint initiatives in field of education.

title: "The Congress on Seeking Solutions to Crises in Europe and Central Asia" Held at IZU
content: "The Congress on Seeking Solutions to Crises in Europe Central Asia" Held at IZU 17.11.2025 At congress, solution proposals for political, social, economic crises experienced across a vast geography stretching from Turkistan to Balkans were discussed, a search for a common vision was evaluated within framework of "ASRICA" Islamic Union Model." Held at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, “Congress on Seeking Solutions to Crises in Region of European Central Asian Islamic States” began with participation of representatives from twelve countries a large audience. The opening of congress, organized by Justice Defenders Strategic Research Center (ASSAM), was attended by many institutional organizational representatives, including Kütahya Dumlupinar University Rector Prof. Süleyman Kiziltoprak, International Balkan University Rector Prof. Lütfi Sunar, Erzurum Atatürk University Rector Prof. Ahmet Hacimüftüoglu, Gaziantep Islamic Science Technology University Rector Prof. Sehmus Demir, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Vice Rector Prof. Erhan Içener, AK Party Deputy Chairman of Election Affairs Prof. Veysel Ayhan, ASSAM President Melih Tanriverdi, TGTV President Ismail Tugrul, AfroAsia Universities Union Director Prof. Ashraf Abdel Rafea Al Darfaili, Islamic Scholars Foundation Board Member Prof. Mustafa Karatas, ASDER Board Member Cemal Erdogan, Union of NGOs of Islamic World Board Member Ibrahim Altan. The congress addressed political instabilities, identity border disputes, major-power competition in Turkistan, along with efforts to find solutions to religious, political, ethnic issues in Cyprus Balkans. Throughout sessions, possibilities of establishing a new vision for region’s countries within framework of ASRICA Islamic Union Model were discussed. In addition to Türkiye, representatives of civil society organizations from twelve countries including Qatar, Malaysia, Kosovo, Sudan shared their observations field experiences. The program also featured contributions from academics from participating countries, who provided scientific perspectives on topic.

title: Sumud Activists Meet with IZU Students
content: Sumud Activists Meet with IZU Students 17.11.2025 Sumud Fleet activists Ikbal Gürpinar Umut Aslan, who set out to draw attention to Israel’s genocide in Gaza to break blockade, met with IZU students. The program “Sumud Experiences from Travelers of Resistance,” organized by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University’s Ahdimiz Kudüs Platform , was held at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The event featured Sumud activists Ikbal Gürpinar Umut Aslan as speakers was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Nihat Erdogmus, faculty members, students. ACAR: “THE ISLAMIC WORLD IS THE TARGET OF THE WEST” Opening discussion, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar highlighted that with collapse of bipolar world in 1990s, West set its sights on Islamic world. Acar noted that despite difficulties faced, Sumud Fleet represented a very important initiative thanked all activists who risked their lives on this dangerous journey. He continued: “Zionist Israel continues its attacks despite ceasefire, its patrons openly maintain their support. Over two billion Muslims have not gone beyond merely observing what has happened over past two years. Our country has certain initiatives, many of which remain out of public eye; however, they are not sufficient. This is because ummah has not achieved unity.” GÜRPINAR: “I DID NOT HESITATE WHEN I WAS INVITED TO JOIN SUMUD” Television host author Ikbal Gürpinar , who described herself as a mufti’s daughter an imam hatip high school graduate, admitted that she became well-informed about Palestine, Jerusalem, Al-Aqsa later in life. She shared her memories from trips to Palestine her Sumud experience. Gürpinar asked participants: “How can you tell if a country is free?” She explained that Palestinians who have no army, currency, industry, or travel rights derive their freedom from their strong faith. Reflecting on her invitation to join Sumud Fleet, she said: “A few weeks earlier, I had broken my shoulder, my arm was in a sling. Yet I did not hesitate to participate. My only question was: ‘Will they accept me?’ We were supposed to depart next day, but we were held in Tunisia for 13 days. Our captains were threatened, their licenses revoked, our ships sabotaged. On day of departure, only 13 of 50 ships could set sail. Everyone had a single goal: to break blockade.” ASLAN: “DON’T LET GAZA FALL OFF YOUR AGENDA” Businessman Sumud activist Umut Aslan said that a new path of resistance has emerged over past two years. He explained that Gaza resistance marked a turning point in his life, noting that he has been to Jerusalem twice in last two years now divides his life into “before Jerusalem” “after Jerusalem.” Pointing to Türkiye’s important role in struggles of Palestinians other oppressed peoples, Aslan advised young people: “Do not let Gaza fall off your agenda. Make space for one more file in your daily schedule name it ‘Jerusalem Gaza.’ Pass on this cause which you support today to your children as well.”

title: The Future of Family Businesses Discussed at IZU
content: The Future of Family Businesses Discussed at IZU 17.11.2025 The Family Business Sustainability Workshop was held at IZU. At international workshop organized in cooperation with Turkish Academy of Sciences (TÜBA) Kyoto Sangyo University, senior representatives from Türkiye’s leading owned companies Ziylan Group, Yildiz Holding, Sinpas Real Estate Investment Trust, Eksim Holding, Kale Group, Hayat Holding shared their institutionalization stories experiences with participants. The international workshop titled “Sustainability Family Business: From Past to Present,” jointly organized by TÜBA Kyoto Sangyo University, took place at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University’s Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The workshop, which focused on sustainability of businesses, was attended by TÜBA President Prof. Dr. Muzaffer Seker, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, TÜBA Sustainable Development Finance Working Group Coordinator IZU Faculty Member Prof. Dr. Mehmet Bulut, IZU Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Nihat Erdogmus, Ziylan Group Chairman Mehmet Büyükeksi, former Chairman of Yildiz Holding Ali Ülker, Board Member of Sinpas REIT Mahmut Çelik, Eksim Holding Chief Financial Officer Bora Çermikli, Kale Group Senior Advisor Yalçin Yilmazkaya, Hayat Holding Vice President for Eurasia Americas Enes Çizmeci, along with academics businesspeople from Türkiye abroad. BULUT: THE WORLD’S OLDEST FAMILY BUSINESS IS IN JAPAN Speaking at opening, Prof. Dr. Mehmet Bulut reminded participants businesses around world face sustainability challenges alongside financial environmental issues. Noting oldest business in world is located in Japan, Bulut added: “This six hundred year old company in Japan is followed by others established in France, Italy, Germany. The oldest business in Türkiye is Sekerci Hacibekir, founded in eighteenth century. The average lifespan of companies in our country is about thirty years. Their most significant challenge is sustainability.” IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar also emphasized uncontrolled consumption has pushed natural resources to brink of depletion since Industrial Revolution. “The vast majority of businesses begin as enterprises. Those overcome sustainability challenges through institutionalization continue to exist,” he said. “SUSTAINABILITY IS AS IMPORTANT FOR SOCIETIES AS IT IS FOR INDIVIDUALS” TÜBA President Prof. Dr. Muzaffer Seker stated workshop aims to address question of “How can businesses preserve their strengths values sustain their existence?” He added: “Sustainability is important not only for individuals but also for societies. Through examples from countries such as Türkiye, Japan, Finland, Germany, Indonesia, we will identify local global factors contribute to success.” FROM ONE PAIR OF SHOES A DAY TO 100,000 PAIRS DAILY In workshop’s first session, senior representatives of Türkiye’s leading businesses shared their experiences. Ziylan Group Chairman Mehmet Büyükeksi described how his uncle Ahmet Muktad Ziylan started company sixty-five years ago in Gaziantep by producing one pair of shoes per day, how it has grown into a company with 800 stores in 24 countries across three continents, producing 100,000 pairs daily in 2023. “Our guiding principles have never changed: hard work, honesty, innovation, work ethic, a sense of responsibility to our customers. Ziylan Group grew around these values,” he said. ÜLKER: THE GROWTH OF COMPANIES IS SIMILAR TO HUMAN DEVELOPMENT Former Chairman of Yildiz Holding, Ali Ülker, summarized eighty-year journey of Ülker brand founded by his grandfather Sabri Ülker in 1944. He noted Ülker has always embraced principle “Make people happy, you will be happy.” Comparing companies’ growth stages to human life, Ülker said, “A child who learns to walk advances with baby steps frequent falls. Family businesses also do not begin professionally; they learn gain experience through trial error.” ÇELIK: WE PRODUCE NOT ONLY BUILDINGS BUT ALSO TRUST Sinpas Holding Board Member Mahmut Çelik said business, founded by his uncle Avni Çelik in 1974, has expanded beyond construction into energy tourism. Citing Avni Çelik’s words, “We build not only buildings but also trust,” he highlighted importance of values passed down through generations noted early professionalization played a key role in their sustained success. ÇERMIKLI: 90% OF FAMILY BUSINESSES DO NOT SURVIVE Eksim Holding CFO Bora Çermikli began by recalling Tivnikli started with flour production in 1980s now exports flour to more than forty countries. The group entered energy sector in 2000s is now Türkiye’s third-largest wind energy producer. Noting rapidly growing institutions inevitably face challenges, he emphasized management sustainability are most critical. He also stated 90% of businesses in Türkiye do not survive beyond second or third generation, underscoring importance of workshop. “ANYONE CAN DO THE EASY THING YOU TRY THE HARD ONE!” Kale Group Senior Advisor Yalçin Yilmazkaya shared story of Kale Group, founded in 1957 in Çanakkale’s Çan district. He noted founder, Ibrahim Bodur, adopted principle of reflecting profit back into society. Quoting Bodur’s advice, “Anyone can do easy thing try hard one,” Yilmazkaya said this principle guides Kale Group’s investments in areas such as turbojet engines national infantry rifles. WHAT ROLE WILL THE NEXT GENERATIONS PLAY? The final speaker of opening session was Hayat Holding Board Member Enes Çizmeci, a fourth-generation representative of group. He explained company founded by Kigili brothers in 1937 now operates in seventeen countries. “The vital question for us is this: our grandfathers founded business, our fathers manage it what will third fourth generations do?” he said, highlighting importance of strong values, education, gaining experience. “As companies institutionalize, we too have become professional employees. We report our work remain accountable,” he added. In afternoon sessions, participants discussed sustainability perspectives of businesses their transformation from past to present, drawing on examples from around world.

title: Innovations and Applications of Artificial Intelligence Discussed at IZU
content: Innovations Applications of Artificial Intelligence Discussed at IZU 19.11.2025 At a program organized by Department of Computer Engineering, innovations application examples of artificial intelligence ranging from healthcare to space technologies, architecture to vehicle safety were thoroughly explored. The “Artificial Intelligence Social Transformation” seminar, hosted by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Department of Computer Engineering, took place in Historical Building’s Museum Hall. In program, where IZU faculty members Prof. Dr. Tarik Özkul Assoc. Prof. Dr. Cevat Resit served as speakers, students from Türkiye, Morocco, France presented project examples demonstrating use of artificial intelligence. The first talk of program was delivered by IZU Computer Engineering Faculty Member Prof. Dr. Tarik Özkul. Stating that he works with artificial intelligence both as an academic as a technology company executive, Özkul shared with students innovations introduced by five AI technologies implemented by his company. The AI applications developed by Özkul’s company operating at IZU Zaim Technopark, honored with numerous international awards, are used in fields of healthcare, wind turbines, satellite technologies, structural health monitoring, solar energy. Highlighting that “Artificial intelligence offers effective, fast, practical possibilities for problem detection solution development,” Özkul noted that they achieved 97% success in AI-supported vertigo treatment. Using Artificial Intelligence to Detect Conflicts of Interest In his talk titled “A Blockchain NLP-Based Multi-Layered Framework for Conflict of Interest Detection in Peer Review,” Assoc. Prof. Dr. Cevat Resit presented their systematic literature review on technological procedural approaches to conflict of interest management. Resit emphasized that conflict of interest remains a persistent issue in scientific peer review, as undisclosed or hidden relationships can undermine fairness. He provided following information about blockchain-based framework they propose: “Our systematic literature review reveals advances in metadata-based conflict of interest detection, reviewer assignment optimization, ethical frameworks, while also highlighting persistent gaps such as limited transparency in context-aware relationship identification, multi-dependency tracking, reporting mechanisms. The framework we propose addresses these limitations offers a multilayered conflict of interest detection model.” The full-day program concluded with presentations delivered by participants including guest students from Morocco France on following topics: “Peer Review Systems Conflicts of Interest” “From Draft to Publication: A Practical Guide for Successful Research Publishing” “Modeling Simulation of Respiratory System During Pressure-Controlled Mechanical Ventilation” “Optimizing Energy Efficiency in Buildings Using AI-Supported Digital Twins”

title: Taha Kilinç Spoke About East Turkestan at IZU
content: Taha Kilinç Spoke About East Turkestan at IZU 19.11.2025 Journalist author Taha Kilinç, who documented trip to East Turkestan-where religious cultural identity is being systematically eroded under intense pressure from China region remains largely isolated from world shared experiences of Uyghur people during talk at IZU. Kilinç was guest of program "Our Route: East Turkestan." During talk, which was also attended by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, he shared observations from visit to region, based on book "In Pursuit of a Lost Geography: A Travelouge of East Turkestan." ACAR: "East Turkestan Is a Bleeding Wound of Islamic World" In opening speech, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar stated East Turkestan is one of bleeeding wounds of Islamic geography. Acar said: "The issue of East Turkestan is a topic world does not pay enough attention to. twenty million of our fellow believers kinsmen are under servere oppression. It is crucial to employ every possible means to make their voices heard to ease their suffering." KILINÇ: “We Were Asked Same Questions Israel Asks at Border” Speaking at talk moderated by SKS Department Head Hülya Sekerci, Taha Kilinç explained although he has been studying Islamic world for years, he was unable to travel to East Turkestan due to China’s visa entry restrictions. When he learned last year grey green passport holders no longer required visas, he began preparing for trip. Kilinç shared experiences from eight-day journey across 15 cities: “We were stopped at every step. The most common questions we faced were: ‘Who are you?’, ‘How do you know about these places?’, ‘Do you know anyone here?’ These were almost identical to questions Israel asks people traveling to Palestine.” Kilinç stressed security measures in region have reached extraordinary levels: “On poles placed every 10–15 meters, there are five or six cameras see in all directions record sound. Chinese officials identify families stay in their homes for 10–15 days for inspection. Those deemed necessary are taken to camps ‘trained.’” “Mosques Are Closed, Hijab Is Banned!” Pointing to silence in streets, anxiety of people, significant obstacles to foreigners entering region, Kilinç stated East Turkestan has been isolated from world for 15 years. He noted he did not see a single woman wearing hijab during visit emphasized China’s social engineering project designed to detach Uyghurs from their religion culture. He listed several restrictions: “It is forbidden to purchase tents, binoculars, telescopes, or ropes above a certain length. A Uyghur cannot stay away from home for more than three days. Mosques are closed, call to prayer is not allowed. Those under 18 cannot enter mosques, but in practice, we did not see anyone under 60 being allowed inside. The last Eid prayer in Kashgar was performed in 2016.” In program, which drew great interest from students, Kilinç stated China uses its economic power to block potential solutions emphasized civil society organizations must pressure governments into action. Following talk, Kilinç signed books at TDV Book Café.

title: “Architecture and Context” Discussed at IZU
content: “Architecture and Context” Discussed at IZU 24.11.2025 The program “Architecture and Context,” organized by the IZU Department of Architecture, was held at the Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The event featured Assoc. Prof. Dr. Rifat Gökhan Koçyigit , faculty member at Mimar Sinan Fine Arts University, Department of Architecture, and was attended by IZU architecture faculty members and students. Koçyigit analyzed context , one of the fundamental concepts of architectural theory, within a broad framework ranging from the philosophy of language to epistemology, and from urban planning to design processes, addressing both its semantic and pragmatic dimensions. “Reality cannot always be grasped directly; it reveals different faces depending on circumstances. Therefore, an architect must learn to doubt, question, and interpret context from multiple perspectives,” he said. Koçyigit also emphasized that the physical context consisting of elements such as solid void balance, texture, scale, and urban organization serves as a key determinant of any architectural project.

title: The Effects of Diabetes on Professional Life Were Discussed
content: The Effects of Diabetes on Professional Life Were Discussed 24.11.2025 At symposium titled "Diabetes Well Being at Work: Know More, Do More," organized by IZU Department of Nursing, relationship between diabetes professional life was evaluated within a scientific framework. Hosted by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Faculty of Health Sciences, Department of Nursing, symposium took place at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall with participation of academics students. ACAR: HEALTHY INDIVIDUALS ARE THE FOUNDATION OF A COUNTRY'S WORKFORCE CAPACITY Delivering opening speech of symposium, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar stated that diabetes has become a global health issue. Acar said: "Particularly in recent years, data show that diabetes is becoming increasingly prevalent in society. Factors such as an aging population, reduced physical activity, unbalanced nutrition significantly contribute to this rise. This indiates that diabetes is not merely a medical issue; it directly affects employee health, workforce productivity, economic performance of counries. Healthy individuals are fundamental component of a nation's workforce capacity. Moreover, development of digital technologies transformation of working models have made management of chronic diseases even more crucial." BAHÇEÇIK: DIABETES IS NOT JUST A MEDICAL ISSUE Dean of Faculty of Health Sciences, Prof. Dr. Ayse Nefise Bahçeçik, emphasized that diabetes is not solely a medical matter, especially within today's repidly changing work models. She noted: "Individuals within diabetes spectrum make up significant portion of global workforce. It is essential to examine relationship between diabetes working life from a s scientific perspective. Diabetes Management in workplace encompasses a wide range of areas, including corporate health policies, workplace based training programs, promotion of healthy lifestyle behaviors, stress burnout management, interdisciplinary monitoring models." As part of program, Prof. Dr. Taner Damci from Istanbul University Cerrahpasa Medical Faculty Hospital presented "Diabetes Working Life: 2025 Perspective," Specialist Nurse Alev Kahraman presented “Nursing Support in Return-to-Work Process for Individuals with Diabetes,” Nurse Tugba Yola delivered a presentation on “The Importance of Nutrition Exercise in Workplace,” Prof. Dr. Zeynep Osar Siva shared her presentation titled “Diabetes Monitoring Through Digital Health Technologies.”

title: IZU Leadership Change
content: IZU Leadership Change 22.10.2025 At Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, Vice Rector Prof. Ibrahim Güney handed over his position to Prof. Nihat Erdogmus. The handover ceremony was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Ismail Küçük, Prof. Erhan Içener, and IZU General Secretary Fatih Hasdemir. During the ceremony, Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar presented a plaque to Prof. Ibrahim Güney, thanking him for his contributions to the university, and wished Prof. Nihat Erdogmus success in his new role.

title: IZU Students Condemn the Genocide in Gaza on the Second Anniversary of October 7th
content: IZU Students Condemn Genocide in Gaza on Second Anniversary of October 7th 22.10.2025 On Second anniversary of October 7th, IZU students protested Israel's attacks on Gaza. Gathering at university's campus in Halkali, students held a march issued a press statement, calling for concete steps to stop genocide. Since start of Israel's heavy bombardment of Gaza following October 7, 2023, Al-Aqsa Flood operation, attacks have escalated to genocidal levels, sparking worldwide protests. On this second anniversary, IZU students gathered at Halkali campus to condemn Israel's atrcities in Gaza. "Sumud: An Expression of Humanity's Shared Values" Students, who marched from ISEFAM building, made their press statement at IZU Square. IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors, faculty members joined students. The statement highlighted Sumud fleet, which set sail with volunteers from 44 countries, as a symbol of resistance shared human values. The intervention against fleet was condemned, with statement stating: “People facing poverty, thirst, hunger under Israeli oppression are waiting hopelessly for international aid, while struggling against death. The countries complicit in this genocide, led by US, have forgotten human values for their own interests, becoming accomplices in this atrocity. It's time for concrete steps from Islamic countries to stop these brutal attacks.” Gazan Student: Silence Neutrality Must End Gazan student Ibrahim Reyen reminded that solidarity shouldn’t just be about slogans. He stated, “Let’s stand together against this machine of oppression. Let’s shout to world: ‘Gaza is not alone. We are with it until occupation ends Palestine is free from sea to river!’ There should be no silence anymore. There should be no neutrality in face of spilled blood.” Acar: Humanity is Watching Gaza Massacre Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar expressed that world has been watching ongoing Israeli oppression since 1948 and, for last two years, genocide with helplessness. He said, “Humanity is watching Gaza massacre. International unity values have lost all meaning. Gaza’s people continue to sacrifice their lives to preserve humanity.” He thanked students for their sensitivity to Gaza encouraged them to work hard to excel in every field. Panel: “A Turning Point in Conflict: From October 7th to Today” IZU’s Political Science Club held a panel discussing impact of October 7th attack. Dr. Fadi Zatari spoke about Israeli propaganda, Dr. Selim Sezer highlighted issues faced by Palestinians outside Gaza. Sezer emphasized need for an equitable solution beyond two-state solution, focusing on supporting Palestinians’ right to determine their own fate. Dr. Zatari criticized labeling of anyone who opposes Israel’s illegal occupation of Palestine as anti-Semitic explained how Israel uses terms like ‘threat’, ‘holocaust’, ‘victim’ to justify its actions suppress discussions on Palestinian rights.

title: Deputy Minister Mahmut Gürcan Discussed Turkey’s e-Commerce and Entrepreneurship Vision at IZU
content: Deputy Minister Mahmut Gürcan Discussed Turkey’s e-Commerce Entrepreneurship Vision at 22.10.2025 At "Digital Economy Entrepreneurship Meeting" hosted by Zaim Technopark, role of e-commerce entrepreneurship in Turkey's economy, innovative business models, global competition strategies were discussed during digital transformation process. Deputy Minister of Trade Mahmut Gürcan was guest speaker at "Turkey's e-Commerce Entrepreneurship Vision" program held at Campus. The event, organized in collaboration with Zaim Technopark, Entrepreneurship Leadership Application Research Center, Entrepreneurship Club, E-Commerce Club, took place at Zaim Technopark Conference Hall. It was attended by Vice Rector Technopark General Manager Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Director of Entrepreneurship Center Dr. Tugçenur Ekinci Furtana, academics, students. The event covered developments in e-commerce entrepreneurship , oppurtunities for new entrepreneurs, initiatives for tech entrepreneurs, planning around cargo return processes. KÜÇÜK: “WE WANT TO INSPIRE YOUNG PEOPLE TO INVENT NEW THINGS” In his speech during program, Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Vice Rector, emphasized that e-commerce e-export are two of most important dynamic topics in today’s economy, pointing out how digital marketplaces open new opportunities for entrepreneurs. He stated, “A product made in Anatolia is being distributed worldwide through innovations created by young people. This dynamic process greatly contributes to national economy. Through such meetings, we want to inspire young people who will ‘invent new things’.” GÜRCAN: "AS TECHNOLOGY ADVANCES, LAWS LAG BEHIND" Deputy Minister Mahmut Gürcan mentioned that it’s possible to divide world into two parts—before after pandemic. He highlighted how e-commerce experienced rapid growth during this period. He said, “It wasn’t just technological advancements that accelerated; entire trade process, including customer behaviors, changed. By end of 2024, Turkey’s e-commerce volume will reach 3 trillion TL, which is about 6.5% of Gross Domestic Product. E-commerce is a rapidly growing dynamic sector. There are critical aspects, like ensuring fair competition protecting consumer rights. As technology progresses, laws are lagging behind. We are working to ensure that legal framework accommodates these changes.” DEPUTY MINISTER MAHMUT GÜRCAN MEETS WITH RECTOR Following "Turkey’s e-Commerce Entrepreneurship Vision" program at Zaim Technopark, Deputy Minister Mahmut Gürcan was hosted by Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar. Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük Prof. Dr. Nihat Erdogmus also attended meeting, during which information was shared about university's entrepreneurship, R&D, Technopark activities.

title: Patent and Trademark Disputes Discussed
content: Patent Trademark Disputes Discussed 22.10.2025 At patent trademark dispute panel organized by Faculty of Law at IZU, changes brought about by artificial intelligence in intellectual property its future impacts were discussed. The "Patent Trademark Disputes from a Practitioner's Perspective" panel, organized by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Faculty of Law, took place at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The events was attended by Prof. Dr. M. Zeki Durak, President of Turkish Patent Trademark Office, Prof, Dr. Erhan Içener, Vice Rector of IZU, Dr. Halit Serhan Ercivelek, Vice Dean of Faculty of Law, Prof. Dr. Canan Küçükali, judges, academics, students. Experts in field discussed current Supreme Court rulings legal issues encountered in practice within framework of Intellectual Industrial Property Law. Dr. Halit Serhan Ercivelek, Vice Dean of Faculty of Law, expressed his pleasure in hosting such an important event. DURAK: "Brand Value Surpasses All Economic Values" Prof. Dr. M. Zeki Durak, President of Turkish Patent Trademark Office, highlighted that Turkey is second country after China in terms of geographic indications ranked twelfth in patent applications. He emphasized, “Our main agenda as an institution is technology investments legal regulations. Industrial property is becoming increasingly important every day. Brand value has surpassed all other economic values.” IÇENER: "Patents Trademarks Are Key to Turkey's Development" Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, Vice Rector of IZU, also stressed that patents, trademarks, geographic indications are among most important indicators of Turkey's development. He added, “These topics are key to inter-country competition. Such meetings bring new perspectives to problems faced in field contribute to solutions. We are aware of importance of issue are happy to be part of this search for solutions.” CALBOLI: "The Future Will Be Defined by AI Intellectual Property" Prof. Dr. Irene Calboli, guest speaker from Texas A&M University, addressed topics of artificial intelligence intellectual property ownership in her presentation titled “Commercial Trademark Patent Disputes in 2025.” She pointed out fundamental conflicts between protectability of AI-generated content intellectual property responsibility rules. She noted that this trend leads to intersections between copyright, patent, data protection laws. Calboli also emphasized that trademark disputes are not only legal issues but also sociological ones, stating, “The future will be defined by artificial intelligence intellectual property issues.” Throughout day, experts in field explored trademark patent disputes through practical examples.

title: STK's and Student Clubs Meet at IZÜ Campus
content: STK's Student Clubs Meet at IZÜ Campus 22.10.2025 Organized in collaboration Turkey Volunteer Associations Foundation (TGTV), Union of Civil Society Organizations of Islamic World (IDSB), Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, STKFest’25 kicked off at IZÜ Stadium. The opening ceremony of event, theme 'Support Gaza,' was attended by Istanbul Governor Davut Gül, Consul General of Bangladesh in Istanbul Muhammad Mizanur Rahman, IZÜ Board of Trustees Chairman Özkan Göksal, Deputy Chairmen Mustafa Büyükabaci Israfil Kisla, Istanbul University-Cerrahpasa Rector Prof. Dr. Nuri Aydin, Haliç University Rector Prof. Dr. Nihat Inanç, IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, Prof. Dr. Nihat Erdogmus, IZÜ Secretary General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir, Istanbul Provincial Director of National Education Murat Mücahit Yentür, Küçükçekmece District Governor Mustafa Anteplioglu, Küçükçekmece District Director of National Education Emin Çikrikçi, TGTV Founders Board Chairman Hamza Akbulut, TGTV Executive Management Board Chairman Ismail Tugrul, IDSB General Secretary Eyüp Akbal, along STK executives, academics, students. The event, which was combined IZÜ's 15th Club Opening Festival, brought together student clubs from Ibn Haldun, Istanbul Commerce, Haliç Universities TGTV-member STKs public institutions. The program aims to strengthen cooperation STKs, promote volunteerism, build a culture of social solidarity. A Hope Tree Was Planted Gaza The festival kicked off “Planting a Hope Tree Gaza” “Silence March Gaza” events, attended by Governor Davut Gül protocol members, along 29 STKs, public institutions, student clubs from universities. In opening speech, IZÜ Student Affairs Clubs Department Head Hülya Sekerci thanked all participants, saying, "In an era where individuality is emphasized, we are searching ways to be 'together'." TGTV Executive Management Board Chairman Ismail Tugrul also expressed hope that this first edition of STKFest would become a tradition, highlighting importance of walking together to connect youth. He said, "We are seeking answers to question 'How can we grow together?' Here, youth are hosts, STKs are guests." Rector Acar: The Active Role of STKs is Important Contribution of Society to Public Administration IDSB General Secretary Eyüp Akbal stated that "STKFest is not just an event, but an awakening of thought conscience" expressed satisfaction in uniting energy of youth conscience of STKs. TGTV Founders Board Chairman Hamza Akbulut reminded that we are heirs of a foundation civilization. IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar then spoke, emphasizing that one of functions of universities is to serve society. He noted that IZÜ has been acting this awareness 15 years. Acar continued, "The active presence of STKs in a country is important contribution of society to public administration. But perhaps equally important is role they play in providing services that governments are unable to deliver, both domestically internationally. Their mission during genocide, which we have been horrified to witness past two years, is clearest evidence of this." Governor Gül: We Will Continue to Uphold 'World Is Bigger Than Five' Claim Istanbul Governor Davut Gül pointed out significance of festival, which was held first time this year. He said, "As in our tradition, civil society awareness is very high today. Through STKs, we reach all corners of world, standing by those in need oppressed. For STKs to work towards their goals, they need human capital volunteers. This task primarily falls on all of us, especially youth. Your support is crucial significant continuing claim 'The World Is Bigger Than Five,' as expressed by our President." After ribbon-cutting ceremony, protocol visited booths set up by nearly 40 institutions, STKs, student clubs. The first day of festival concluded a piano recital, a poetry competition using artificial intelligence, other activities. The festival will continue on its second day competitions, award ceremonies, a "Brotherhood Dinner.

title: IZÜ Participated in the 15th UNSA Study Expo Held in Sarajevo
content: IZÜ Participated in the 15th UNSA Study Expo Held in Sarajevo 27.10.2025 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZÜ) participated in the 15th UNSA Study Expo , hosted by the University of Sarajevo . As part of the event, the IZÜ delegation visited the University of Sarajevo and met with Vice-Rector Prof. Dr. Kemal Duric to exchange views on enhancing academic and cultural cooperation between the two institutions. The fair brought together 49 institutions from 21 different countries , providing a platform for establishing various connections and collaborations related to international partnerships and student exchange programs.

title: The Delegation from Malaysia Tawau Teacher Education Institute Visited IZU
content: The Delegation from Malaysia Tawau Teacher Education Institute Visited IZU 03.11.2025 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University hosted an academic delegation from Malaysia Tawau Teacher Education Institute. The delegation, led by Abdul Hamid bin Abdul Rahim, Head of Department of Strategic Programs, Research and Innovation, included Richeal Phil Thien Kim How, Head of International Relations Unit; Hajah Rosnah Abdul Razak, Program Assistant; Jamilah binti Ahmad, Head of STEM Department; Hajah Latifah binti Monnas, Head of Educational Sciences Department; and Lim Fui Chiew, Head of Physical Education Department. During meeting, attended by IZU Rector Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Ismail Küçük and Prof. Nihat Erdogmus, Dean of Faculty of Education Prof. Ahmet Sükrü Özdemir, and Vice Dean Prof. Emrah Görgülü, topics under theme of "Sharing Excellence and Research Publishing” were discussed. The meeting focused on sharing best practices and enhancing academic collaboration between two institutions.

title: The "Philosophy for Children" Program was held at IZÜ
content: "Philosophy for Children" Program was held at IZÜ 27.10.2025 "4th philosophy for Children Workshop," organized by New Turkey Education Foundation (YETEV) in collaboration with Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZÜ), focused on theme of "Philosophy Family" this year. importance of family in raising individuals who think critically reflectively was emphasized during program. "4th Philosophy for Children Workshop" (ÇIF), organized by YETEV IZÜ, took place at IZÜ Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. workshop featured speakers Prof. Yaylagül Ceran Karatas from Istanbul Medeniyet University Prof. Enis Doko from Ibn Haldun University. event was attended by IZÜ Rector Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Erhan Içener, YETEV Shools General Manager Hayati Oktay, administrators teachers from workshop was "Philosophy Family." OKTAY: THE FAMILY IS THE BEGINNING SHCOOL OF PHILOSOPHY In his opening speech, YETEV Schools General Manager Hayati Oktay highlighted "Philosophy for Children" lessons, which started in 2021, now cover all students from second to seventh grade at YETEV-affiliated shcools. Referring to Newton's questioning of why an apple falls to ground, Oktay pointed out how this simple question changed course of world. He continued: "Since Socrates, humanity has been seeking answers to question 'why'. first institution where this question found meaning is family, starting school of philosophy. Our goal is to raise students who ask, question, approach life with critical thinking." ACAR: IT IS IMPORTANT TO KNOW WHERE WE ROOT OURSELVES Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Rector of IZÜ, emphasized negative impact on children's intellectual mental development when their questions are left unanswered. He stated it is impossible for someone suspends their critical thinking to become a good person, good citizen, or good Muslim. Reminding philosophy is not limited to activities of ancient Greek thinkers, Acar emphasized importance of guiding people from our own beliefs cultural world. He said, "It is important where we root ourselves. We have all seen where Western civilization has led humanity. values of Islamic civilization must be passed on to new generations, this is more important than we might think." KARATAS: THE FAMILY IS A WAY OF BEING TOGETHER first speaker of workshop, Prof. Yaylagül Ceran Karatas from Istanbul Medeniyet University, addressed question "What is family?" in her talk titled "What is what is Not Family in Context of Ethics, Political Economy, Technology." She argued family should be considered not only in its social context but also in its existential aspect. "When talking about family, we first need to clear our mindes of informational conceptual pollution," Karatas stated, emphasizing family cannot be reduced to a stucture consisting only of a contract between mother, father, child. She noted genocides are not only committed with weapons, we must recognize we have also been subjected to political epistemological genocides. "We need to redefine our concepts before discussing topics, including family," she said. She also pointed out family, which was defined as an institution after Industrial Revolution, cannot be reduced to this definition, Western-centric political economy technological developments have transformed family. " family is a form of relationship, a way of being together. It forms a larger structure includes neighborhood. To create a world, we must first define our own concepts," Karatas concluded. PHILOSOPHY FOR CHILDREN TALKS Following opening speech, "Philosophy for Children (ÇIFx) Talks" session discussed role of family in raising thinking individuals transformative effects of digital age on concept of a family. session featured Doç. Ridvan Karabulut, Director of Social Sciences Vocational School at Kayseri University, Doç. Esra Ileri Türk, a faculty member at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University's Faculty of Islamic Sciences. After session, participants moved on to workshop activities. workshops, which included topics such as “Responsibility Trust in Family,” “Critical Thinking in Digital World: Ways to Guide Children,” “The Silent Language of Bond: Role, Value, Inquiry Experience in Family,” “What is Truth Friendship?”, “The Dialectic of Thinking: Problem Solving, Critical Thinking, Decision Making in Age of Artificial Intelligence,” “Thinking About Existence with Adults: Child, Mole, Fox, Horse,” “Oh No, Children Are Asking Questions!”, were followed by a closing session. "BEING A FAMILY IN THE MODERN AGE" guest speaker for closing session was Prof. Enis Doko from Ibn Haldun University’s Department of Philosophy. In his speech titled "The Family as a School of Wisdom," Doko discussed reasons consequences of family increasingly being viewed as a mere contract or temporary choice in modern age. He emphasized family is not just a social institution but also a fundamental environment shapes identity, virtues, spirituality. By comparing views of contemporary philosophers with approaches of Sufism, Doko argued family is a space where human values such as loyalty, compassion, sacrifice, responsibility are learned. workshop concluded with presentation of gifts to workshop trainers participants.

title: Cooperation Agreement Between IZU and Sudan
content: Cooperation Agreement Between IZU Sudan 03.11.2025 The Consul General of Republic of Sudan in Istanbul delegation from Sudanese Community in Istanbul visited our university. The delegation included Osaman Mahjoub, Consul General of Republic of Sudan in Istanbul; Prof. Dr. Nizar Ata, President of Sudanese Community in Istanbul; Naji El Karshabi, Secretary of Media Public Relations Official Spokesperson of Community; Dr. Magda Cafer, Secretary of Women Children's Unit; Oatada Kamal, President of Turkish Sudanese Students Union; Ahmed Ammar, President of Istanbul Sudanese Students Association. The meeting was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar; Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener; Acting Director of IZU TÖMER Lecturer Nurhilal Emre; International Office Coordinator Nesibe Aloudatallah Ghadban. During meeting, strengthening relations between IZU Sudanese community was discussed. The participants also evaluated opportunities for increasing mutual academic cultural exchange explored potential future joint projects. Additionally, a consensus was reached on cooperation in language education through Turkish language courses offered by IZU TÖMER for Sudanese students.

title: 2024–2025 Academic Year General Assembly Meeting Held
content: 2024–2025 Academic Year General Assembly Meeting Held 14.07.2025 The 2024–2025 End-of-Year Academic General Assembly Meeting of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University was held at the Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The meeting was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük and Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, Secretary General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir, and the university’s academic staff.

title: International Graduates Welcomed at IZU Rectorate
content: International Graduates Welcomed at IZU Rectorate 28.08.2025 "At the gathering attended by our Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, and the International Office administration, graduates who had studied at IZU for nearly six years and distinguished themselves through their academic achievements and active participation in social activities expressed their gratitude to our university before returning to their home countries. Rector Acar offered valuable advice for life and extended his best wishes for their future success."

title: Designing Course Syllabi ~ Adaptation Program for the Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences
content: Designing Course Syllabi ~ Adaptation Program for the Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences 18.09.2025 A training program titled “Designing Course Syllabi” was organized on September 17, 2025, by IZU CELT to enhance the professional competence of the academic staff at the Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences. As part of the faculty orientation program, Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kamil Arif Kirkiç delivered the training session, which focused on defining course objectives and learning outcomes, as well as formulating effective learning goals.

title: Assessment and Evaluation Techniques
content: Assessment and Evaluation Techniques 21.05.2025 On May 20, 2025, a training session on assessment and evaluation techniques was held at the Central Library (KT046). Delivered by Assoc. Prof. Dr. Saban Berk, the session explored current assessment methods in educational contexts.

title: Veysel Eroglu Spoke About Abdulhamid II at IZU
content: Veysel Eroglu Spoke About Abdulhamid II at IZU 21.05.2025 Former Minister of Forestry and Water Affairs Prof. Dr. Veysel Eroglu Delivered a Seminar on "Understanding Sultan Abdulhamid II" at IZU Prof. Dr. Veysel Eroglu, former Minister of Forestry and Water Affairs, delivered a seminar titled “ Understanding Abdulhamid II ” at our university. Organized by IZUSEM and held in the Seminar Hall-1 of the Historic Building, the event was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, Secretary General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir, as well as academic and administrative staff and students. During the seminar, the life, political approaches, and historical legacy of Ottoman Sultan Abdulhamid II were discussed in detail. Prof. Dr. Eroglu shared insights into the Sultan’s knowledge and experience in state governance acquired since his education years, the historical perspective he gained during his travels in Europe, and significant anecdotes concerning key developments of the period.

title: "77 Years of Exile" Exhibition on Display at IZU
content: "77 Years of Exile" Exhibition on Display at IZU 21.05.2025 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University is hosting a powerful exhibition that highlights 77-year-long struggle of Palestinian people marked by exile, resistance, perseverance. Organized by Center for Islam Global Affairs (CIGA), exhibition coincides with anniversary of Nakba (the “Great Catastrophe”), which began in 1948 continues to this day. It takes visitors on a deeply moving historical journey. The exhibition opened with participation of IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar CIGA Director Sami Al-Arian, features works by Gazan photographer Abdurrahman Kahoot. Through a compelling display of photographs documents, exhibition traces scars left by forced displacement, destruction, loss, life in refugee conditions. At same time, it visually narrates stories of resistance, identity preservation, unwavering determination to return. Each photograph serves as a fragment of an ongoing timeline, inviting visitors into a story that stretches from past into present. This visual narrative not only informs, but also calls on viewers to remember, reflect, bear witness to Palestinian struggle for freedom. With this exhibition, university aims to remind us that these events are not just part of history, but a living experience that continues to resonate within conscience of humanity.

title: Turkish Language Festival Enthusiasm at IZU
content: Turkish Language Festival Enthusiasm at IZU 21.05.2025 The "1st Turkish Language Festival", organized by IZU TÖMER and Turkish Preparatory Program students, was held at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The event brought together students learning Turkish as a foreign language and their families on our university campus. The festival began with Turkish National Anthem and a moment of silence. Opening speeches were delivered by Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük and Assoc. Prof. Dr. Osman Özdemir. They addressed importance of Turkish language education at IZU, foreign students' sensitivity toward language, educational process, and significance of festival. As part of festival, awards were presented to winners of essay competition on topic: "What kind of life would you have lived if you were born as child of a turkish family in Turkey?" Students also performed poems and songs, and a four-act play was staged. Within scope of Turkish Preparatory Program's "Advisory Student Project", certificates of participation were given to faculty students who mentored international students.

title: Türk Hava Kurumu on Its 100th Anniversary of Establishment
content: Türk Hava Kurumu on Its 100th Anniversary of Establishment 22.05.2025 Kemal Yurtnaç, Chair of Trustee Committee of Türk Hava Kurumu (THK), met with students of IZÜ. During program, where Yurtnaç shared good news that three of our students would be offered internship opportunities at THK, three students were also awarded a glider flight experience another three a paragliding experience through a draw. The latest guest of “Hayatimiza Dokunanlar” (Those Who Make an Impact on Our Lives) series organized by SKS Daire Baskanligi was Vali Kemal Yurtnaç, Chair of Trustee Committee of Türk Hava Kurumu (THK). The opening speeches of program, held at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim Üniversitesi Nizamülmülk Conference Hall, were delivered by Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar SKS Department Head Hülya Sekerci. Sekerci highlighted that under leadership of Kemal Yurtnaç, institution has been restructured in areas such as youth projects, search rescue operations, raising social awareness. Rector Acar emphasized that young people have much to learn from experienced professionals thanked Kemal Yurtnaç for his participation. YURTNAÇ: “Istikbal Göklerdedir” Is Our Motto Starting his speech by stating that they have adopted Mustafa Kemal Atatürk’s words “Istikbal Göklerdedir” (“The future is in skies”) as their motto, Yurtnaç provided audience with information about structure current activities of THK. He also shared with IZÜ students experiences he gained during his service at Presidency for Turks Abroad Related Communities, his time as Governor, now as Chair of Trustee Committee of THK. In addition to announcing internship opportunities for three students, program also featured a draw in which three students won a glider flight three others a paragliding experience.

title: The Use of AI-Supported Teaching Technologies in Instructional Processes
content: The Use of AI-Supported Teaching Technologies in Instructional Processes 02.06.2025 IZU CELT organized a four-session training program on AI-supported instructional technologies. Led by Dr. Özden Karagöz Mirçik, the sessions aimed to equip participants with the knowledge and skills needed to effectively integrate AI-supported instructional tools into their teaching practices. Throughout the sessions, participants explored practical applications and strategies to enhance learning through artificial intelligence.

title: Volkan Demirel Attends “IZÜ Spor Festivali” as Guest of Honor
content: Volkan Demirel Attends “IZÜ Spor Festivali” as Guest of Honor 23.05.2025 The “IZÜ Spor Festivali,” organized by Turkey’s first student-established sports council, took place with participation of former national football player coach Volkan Demirel. The “Spor Festivali,” organized for first time this year by student members of Sports Council under SKS Daire Baskanligi, was held at IZÜ Stadium. The content of festival was shaped according to students' preferences. Organized by Turkey’s first student-established sports council, event featured stations such as sack racing, foot tennis, trampoline, boxing machine, giant chess, an archery platform — all of which attracted strong interest from students. The opening ceremony was attended by IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, Dean of Faculty of Sport Sciences Prof. Dr. Mustafa Zahit Serarslan, Besiktas Congress Member Ahmet Çaglar. Supporting Sports Against Digital Addiction In his opening speech, Rector Acar emphasized importance of physical activity in digital age. He mentioned advantages of sports facilities on IZÜ’s campus for students stated that sports should be encouraged as a means of combating digital addiction. Vice Rector Erhan Içener also expressed that IZÜ administration supports sports activities both financially morally takes pride in organizing such events. Volkan Demirel Shares His Experiences The festival, which featured performances by folk dance, taekwondo, archery teams, also welcomed participation of former national football player, sports commentator, coach Volkan Demirel. Demirel shared his experiences in both his football career coaching journey with youth. During event, awards were also presented to IZÜ football, taekwondo, archery teams for their achievements in inter-university competitions.

title: AI-Supported Technology Usage in Research and Teaching
content: AI-Supported Technology Usage in Research and Teaching 19.06.2025 IZU CELT hosted a training session on the use of AI-supported technologies in research and teaching on June 19, 2025. Led by Dr. Mehmet Akin Bulut, the session explored how AI-based tools can support academic research and enhance classroom instruction. The program included both theoretical explanations and hands-on examples demonstrating effective use of artificial intelligence in academic settings.

title: Important Announcement for Students in the PhD Dissertation Proposal or Dissertation Monitoring Stage in the Fall Semester of 2025-2026
content: Important Announcement for Students in PhD Dissertation Proposal or Dissertation Monitoring Stage in Fall Semester of 2025-2026 01.10.2025 defenses/dissertation meetings for fall semester of 2025-2026 academic year must be held by end of office hours on Wednesday, December 31, 2025, at latest. Students who exceed this deadline will be considered unsuccessful in their proposals/dissertation stage. thesis documents of PhD students in fall semester must be submitted to Graduate Education Institute by Head of Department by Monday, January 5, 2026 , at latest. Click here for Graduate Academic Administrative Calendar. Last day for Dissertation Proposal Defenses/Dissertation Monitoring Meetings December 31, 2025 Last Day for Submission of Dissertation Proposal Defenses/ Dissertation Monitoring Documents January 5, 2026 POINTS TO CONSIDER According to IZU Graduate Education Training Regulations; Dissertation Proposal Defense ARTICLE 23 - (1) student, who successfully completes PhD qualifying exam, defends proposal, which includes purpose, method, study plan of research to be carried out, in front of committee, within six months at latest. delivers a written report on to committee members at least fifteen days before oral defense. (2) committee makes decision on acceptance, correction, or rejection of submitted by with an absolute majority. One month is given for correction. At end of this period, decision made in direction of acceptance or rejection by an absolute majority is notified to Institute by head of department, with a report within three days following end of process. (3) whose is rejected has right to choose a new supervisor and/or subject. In this case, a new committee able to be appointed. who wants to continue program with same supervisor is taken to defense again within three months, who changed supervisor subject within six months. whose is also rejected in this defense is disenrolled from University. time elapsed in these stages is counted from duration of student's PhD education. (5) who does not take defense within time specified in first paragraph without an acceptable excuse is considered unsuccessful, thesis is rejected. Dissertation Monitoring Meetings ARTICLE 23 - (4) For whose thesis is accepted, thesis committee meets at least twice a year, once in January-June once in July-December. submits a written report to committee members at least one month before meeting date. In this report, a summary of study done so far study plan for next semester are stated. student's thesis study is determined as successful or unsuccessful by committee. Students who are found unsuccessful by committee twice in a row or three times intermittently are dismissed from University. thesis report minutes must be submitted by thesis supervisors to Graduate Education Institute with approval of Head of Department through UBYS system. supervisors from outside IZU, must contact relevant Head of Department to submit documents of their students. PhD students who have successfully completed courses, seminars, at least three meetings within their deadlines, have a weighted grade point average of 3,00 above, meet publication requirement can take defense exam.

title: About 2025-2026 Fall Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Schedule
content: About 2025-2026 Fall Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Schedule 20.10.2025 Students who are willing to apply for PhD qualifying exam to be held in Fall semester of 2025-2026 academic year are required to fill out PhD Qualifying application form at https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/login between October 20-24, 2025 . PhD Qualifying Exams will be held on a date to be determined by relevant Head of Departments in December 2025 . The exams will be held at our Halkali Campus. POINTS TO BE CONSIDERED 1- Students who have successfully completed their courses and seminar, have a cumulative grade point average of at least 3.00 and above, and fulfill Foreign Language Proficiency can apply for doctoral qualifying exam. 2- Students admitted with a master's degree must take doctoral qualifying exam until end of fifth semester at latest in compliance with relevant regulations. 3- In order for students to take PhD Qualifying Exam, they must have a thesis supervisor and choose from “PhD Qualifying Exam” section at https://derskayit.izu.edu.tr/Login 4- Students who applied for PhD Qualifying Examination held in Fall semester of 2024-2025 academic year and did not take exam or failed exam, are required to apply again on specified dates. Students who do not apply will be considered unsuccessful and will be disenrolled from University. 5- Please click here for post-exam procedures of students who are successful in PhD qualifying exam.
