title: IZU FOOTBALL TENNIS TEAM BECAME THE SECOND IN ISTANBUL
content: IZU FOOTBALL TENNIS TEAM BECAME THE SECOND IN ISTANBUL 26.04.2017 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Football Tennis Team was 2nd in the Istanbul Universities Football Tennis Tournament. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, which has won numerous trophies in different dances, has added another success to its successes. Turkey University Sports Federation and Mef University arranged this tournament. IZU Football Tennis Team represented our university and achieved a great victory in the category of ‘Football Tennis’. The IZU Football Tennis Team, which has struggled with challenging races throughout its first tournament, was established in 2017.

title: The Final of the ‘Inter-High School Oratory Competition’ Took Place at IZU
content: The Final of ‘Inter-High School Oratory Competition’ Took Place at IZU 01.12.2025 The district final of "Inter-High School Oratory Competition," organized in cooperation with Istanbul Provincial Directorate of National Education Ilim Yayma Cemiyeti, were held at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. The Küçükçekmece district finals of "Inter-High School Oratory Competition," themed "Prof. Sabahattin Zaim Hoca," organized for third time this year, took place in Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The program was attended by IZÜ Rector Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Nihat Erdogmus, Küçükçekmece District National Education Branch Director Rafet Fener, as well as many teachers students. Delivering opening speech, IZÜ Rector Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized importance of using Turkish language accurately effectively. Rector Acar stated, "Our Language holds a vital place in unity integrity of Türkiye. In recent years, shortcomings have arisen such as insufficient value being given to our native language due to focus on foreign language education. Therefore, in order to raise strong competent individuals, we must also be active in social sciences humanities. Developing using Turkish as a language of education science is a national duty.” Küçükçekmece District National Education Branch Director Rafet Fener expressed his satisfaction that competition was hosted by IZÜ. Fener stated: “We are pleased to hold this competition here today, where we will listen to 20 students from 20 high schools in our district. Since theme of our competition is our late teacher Sabahattin Zaim, being at university that bears his name is meaningful for us.” In their speeches, participating students discussed life, ideas, works of Prof. Sabahattin Zaim. As aspiring masters of oratory who embrace Zaim Hoca’s legacy, students showcased their rhetorical skills. The jury members for competition were IZÜ academics Merve Ergüt, Ali Sait Yagar, Nükte Sevim Derdicok, Nurbanu Sahin Hatipoglu, Necmettin Akay, Research Assistant Safura Inan.

title: IZU ARCHERY TEAM WAS SECOND IN TURKEY
content: IZU ARCHERY TEAM WAS SECOND IN TURKEY 15.05.2017 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University returned to university as the Turkey second in the 2017 Turkish Inter-University 70M Archery Championship. 188 archery participated to championship from 52 universities. The championship ended with a final shot and a wide awards ceremony on 13 May Saturday. Archery Competition formed with eight different categories. Women’s Classic Team and Women Roller Team won Turkey Second achievement in the championship. Roller Man’s Team and Roller Mix Team completed to championship as a fourth. Afra Aydin, Fatmanur Özkan and Meryem Kutlu represented to IZU in the Roller Women’s Team and Hilalnur Özçirak, Beyzagül Yildirim and Sümeyye Örs represented to IZU in the Women’s Classic Team. Thus Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Archery Team achieved ‘Second Turkey’ in two different categories.

title: 2022-2023 Spring Semester Graduate Course Selection Procedures
content: 2022-2023 Spring Semester Graduate Course Selection Procedures 01.02.2023 Dear student, You can make your course selection process for spring semester of 2022-2023 academic year between 02-12 February 2023 at https://derskayit.izu.edu.tr/ with your e-mail address password. Students who have completed course period; Doctoral students: Ph.D. Qualification Exam, Ph.D. Thesis Proposal, Ph.D. Thesis Master's students with thesis: Master’s Thesis Master's students with non-thesis: Master’s Project is compulsory to choose course periods. Students who do not renew their registration cannot get a Student Certificate, Discounted Transportation Card, IZU Scholarship or Discount, KYK Scholarship or Credit , in addition, these students' Military Postponement Procedures are not carried out; if they has been made, it is canceled, Residence Permit/Residence Certificate is not given for international students, if a residence permit/residence certificate has been given, it is canceled. Students whose registration is not renewed are updated as "Passive-Unregistered Student" in IZU- YÖK-YÖKSIS Information System, semester/s whose registration is not renewed are counted as student's maximum education period. Other things to consider: Those who do not know e-mail address password of can renew their passwords at https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/login via "Forgot Password " link. Course selections have to be approved by course coordinators. Thesis/Project, Ph.D. Qualification Ph.D. Thesis Proposal selections will be automatically approved by system. Students who do not have a Thesis/Project supervisor must contact Head of Department request a supervisor to be appointed. (A thesis supervisor cannot be selected other than Thesis/Project supervisor, which is listed in "Student Information" section of https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/login address. Students who request a thesis advisor change should contact Department.) If you have any problems during course selection process, you can contact your program supervisor via e-mail. ( Click for e-mail addresses of Program Responsibles.) Students who receive a debt alert during course selection can learn their debt information make payments at https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/odeme . Those who have completed 4 semesters study period in doctoral master's programs with thesis cannot choose a course. Those who have completed 3 semesters study period in non-thesis master's programs cannot choose a course. The starting date of courses is 06 February 2023 . Click for Academic Calendar. *Registration renewal course registration are entirely responsibility of student.

title: Important Announcement for Thesis Master's Students whose Normal-Maximum Study Period Will Expire
content: Important Announcement Thesis Master's Students whose Normal-Maximum Study Period Will Expire 23.03.2023 In Thesis Master's programs of our Institute, supervisors of students, have completed normal study period of four semesters or a maximum study period of six semesters (except Scientific Preparation, Suspension of Registration, Additional Time) as of end of Spring Semester of 2022-2023 academic year, are required to send word version of to Thesis Control Team by end of working hours on Friday, at latest, June 23, 2023. Theses submitted after June 23, 2023, be accepted. defense forms of master students graduate in spring semester be referred by Head of Department to Graduate Education Institute until July 14, 2023, Friday . Students in this situation meet publication requirement before July 14, 2023 . master students graduate in spring semester take defense exam by August 18, 2023, Friday . POINTS TO CONSIDER students have at least seven courses one Seminar course (a total of 60 ECTS) together with a minimum GPA of 2.50 to take defense exam. students enrolled in master's programs fulfil at least one of publication requirements. ( Click here information about publication requirements.) primary responsibility preparation of in accordance with Thesis Writing Guidelines belongs to student supervisor. Thesis Control Team members are responsible formal control of final drafts of theses. supervisor e-mail their student's to Thesis Control Team members. Click here control e-mail addresses. theses that do comply with Thesis Writing Guideline and/or those similarity reports exceed 20% be corrected submitted within five days. Thesis Control Team Members control more than three times. All responsibility in this regard belongs to supervisor student. It be noted that singular similarities exceed 1% in plagiarism report. Supervisors submit documents of master's students graduate to Graduate Education Institute via EBYS system. students have submitted their proposal to Graduate Education Institute through their supervisors be able to take defense exam even if they have completed their thesis. students, are able to complete their until June 23, 2023, within their normal study period of four semesters extend their submission to Fall Semester of 2023-2024 academic year, are required to pay tuition one course extension period. (No discount be applied to tuition fee paid extension period.) This situation is valid Spring Semester of 2023-2024 Academic Year, second extension period. students, have failed in study or have been able to take defense exam within maximum study period of six semesters, be disenrolled from University. According to 1st 2nd paragraphs of article 9 of IZU Graduate Education Training Regulations; (1) duration of master's program with is four semesters, starting from semester in which courses related to program enrolled in program are given, regardless of whether they are registered each semester, except period spent in scientific preparation, program is completed in a maximum of six semesters. (2) student has failed to complete credit courses one Seminar course or has been able to fulfill success criteria determined by University, has failed in study or has been able to take defense exam within maximum period of study be disenrolled from University.

title: About 2022-2023 Spring Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Schedule
content: About 2022-2023 Spring Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Schedule 29.03.2023 Dear doctoral student, Students who ask to apply for doctoral qualifying exam to be held in Spring semester of 2022-2023 academic year are required to fill out Doctoral Qualifying application form at https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/login between Apr il 03-14, 2023 . Doctoral Qualifying Exams will be held in June 2023 on a date determined by relevant Department Heads. Exams will be held at Halkali or Altunizade Campus. POINTS TO CONSIDER 1- Students who have successfully completed their courses and seminar, have a weighted grade point average of at least 3.00 and above, and fulfill foreign language proficiency can apply for doctoral qualifying exam. 2- Students admitted with a master's degree must take doctoral qualifying exam by end of fifth semester at latest in compliance with relevant regulations. 3- Students who ask to take Doctoral Qualifying Examination must have a thesis supervisor and choose from “Doctoral Qualifying Examination” section at https://derskayit.izu.edu.tr/Login 4- Students who applied for Doctoral Qualifying Examination held in fall semester of 2022-2023 academic year and did not take exam or failed exam are required to apply again on specified dates. Students who do not apply will be considered unsuccessful and will be disenrolled from University. 5- Please click here for post-exam procedures of students who are successful in doctoral qualifying exam.

title: 2nd BOWLING TOURNAMENT EXCITEMENT AT IZU
content: 2nd BOWLING TOURNAMENT EXCITEMENT AT IZU 19.12.2017 The Bowling Tournament was organized last year for first time. It was completed with intense participation and beautiful memorials this year. The 2nd IZU Bowling Tournament which was held within scope of Fall Tournaments was also scene for 7D Cinema, Karaoke Rooms, Colliding Otos, Basketball Shooting. At end of tournament, Melisa Bahsi, Merhaba Mehmet and Sebahat Durmus were first three in category of ladies, while Fatih Sahin, Yasin Akdag and Muhammed Younas were in men respectively.

title: Kanat: America at a Crossroads
content: Kanat: America at a Crossroads 01.12.2025 Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kiliç Bugra Kanat, Member of Security Foreign Policies Council of Presidency of Republic of Türkiye, spoke at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University about impact of four major crises United States has experienced over past twenty years on global dynamics. Kanat who is also a faculty member at Penn State University Research Director at SETA-Washington D.C. participated in program organized by IZU Department of Political Science International Relations Politics Club. As guest speaker of conference titled “America’s Longest Twenty Years: The History of America in Four Crises” held at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall, he evaluated how crises U.S. has faced in last two decades have affected state governance foreign policy preferences. Delivering opening remarks, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar underscored importance of hearing such evaluations from an academic who closely observes United States: “Since Second World War, America has been an ally of Türkiye. With its far-reaching global ties, changes happening there leave deep impacts on world politics,” he said. “THE AMERICAN PUBLIC DOES NOT WANT WAR” During session moderated by Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, Kanat discussed how period beginning with 9/11 continuing through Iraq War, 2008 Economic Crisis, ending with 2020 Covid Pandemic has shaped U.S. governance foreign policy. Highlighting American public’s reluctance toward large-scale foreign engagements, Kanat stated, “To understand today’s U.S. policy, one must carefully examine last century.” He explained how society’s inward-looking tendency influenced elections: “The victories of Bill Clinton, Barack Obama, Donald Trump were driven by their focus on domestic issues during their campaigns. The American people do not want foreign policy to overshadow their own concerns.” KANAT: 9/11 HAS NOT ENDED Kanat pointed out that U.S. experienced crises similar to recent ones in early 20th century, but difference today is that these crises do not reach a conclusion. “Japan’s Pearl Harbor attack was a shock similar to 9/11. In response, America dropped atomic bombs on Hiroshima Nagasaki, Pearl Harbor was over. But despite wars in Afghanistan Iraq, 9/11 has not ended. The other crises America has faced in past twenty years also remain unresolved.” “AMERICA IS AT A CROSSROADS” Noting growing anti-war sentiment in U.S. public sphere, Kanat remarked: “America is at a crossroads. There is a strongly anti-Israel faction, but their anger is not about Gaza; it’s about belief that their country has been dragged into war.” He added that Trump’s plan for 2026 elections points toward a more inward-looking foreign policy. “THE WORLD SYSTEM IS CHANGING” Emphasizing that global system is being reshaped, Kanat stated that political landscape is moving toward a multipolar structure reminiscent of pre–World War II era. Addressing Türkiye–U.S. relations, he noted that ties between two countries have been driven by security concerns since beginning. “In every crisis we have experienced with U.S. since Second World War, Congress has played an active role. In records of Sivas Congress, we see Rauf Orbay complaining about negative stance of American Congress, just like today’s politicians,” he said. Kanat identified two additional key factors straining bilateral relations: relations with Russia weapons policies.

title: Kizilay Tent at IZU
content: Kizilay Tent at IZU 01.12.2025 Kizilay conducted a blood donation campaign on the IZU campus between November 24–26. Within the scope of the campaign, carried out under the slogan “Every Donation Saves 3 Lives, Plants 3 Saplings,” students and university staff donated blood. Chairman of the Board of Trustees Özkan Göksal, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, and Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, and Prof. Dr. Nihat Erdogmus also visited the Turkish Red Crescent Blood Donation Tent set up at IZU Square to show their support for the campaign.

title: Personal Data Protection Awareness Training (KVKK)
content: Personal Data Protection Awareness Training (KVKK) 26.11.2025 The “Personal Data Protection Law Awareness Training”, organized by the Human Resources Department, was held in the Historical Building Seminar Hall. Attended by IZU Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener as well as academic and administrative staff, the training focused on the importance of protecting personal data and the duties and responsibilities of data controllers. Topics were discussed in detail within the framework of questions such as: “What is Personal Data, Sensitive Data, and Health Data?”, “What is the Scope of Personal Data?”, and “How Should Personal Data Be Protected?”

title: The seminar titled “The Use of Brick in Architectural Solutions” was held
content: The seminar titled “The Use of Brick in Architectural Solutions” was held 01.12.2025 The event titled “The Advantages of Using Brick in Architectural Solutions”, organized by Department of Architecture, explored structural, aesthetic, and sustainability-focused contributions of brick in architectural design processes. During program, TOKI Business Development and Project Manager Civil Engineer Filiz Bulutlar and Ceramic Engineer & Product Manager Erdem Uslu delivered presentations, sharing comprehensive technical insights ranging from brick production stages to advantages material offers in architectural solutions. In practical session of program, students participated in a wall-building activity under guidance of instructors, gaining hands-on experience that allowed them to apply their theoretical knowledge in field.

title: All Departments of the Faculty of Business and Management Sciences Have Been Accredited by STAR
content: All Departments of the Faculty of Business Management Sciences Have Been Accredited by STAR 03.12.2025 All programs of IZU’s Faculty of Business Management Sciences have been accredited by STAR (Social Humanities Fundamental Sciences Accreditation Rating Association). Following the application submitted for all programs that graduated students in 2024, comprehensive on-site off-site evaluations were conducted. As a result, the International Trade Finance (Turkish–English), Islamic Economics Finance (Turkish–English), Business Administration (English), Economics (English) programs were deemed eligible to receive STAR accreditation, which represents quality assurance. The accreditation of all departments within the faculty is significant as it demonstrates that the quality of education, program outcomes, faculty qualifications, infrastructure opportunities, student support, assessment–evaluation processes, institutional operations fully meet the national international standards set by STAR.

title: Alternative Methods in Education
content: Alternative Methods in Education 03.12.2025 IZÜ Primary Mathematics Education Department organized the "Alternative Methods in Education" program at the Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The event featured teacher and educator Talha Boyalik as the keynote speaker and was attended by Education Faculty Dean Prof. Dr. Ahmet Sükrü Özdemir, Vice Dean Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kamil Arif Kirkiç, academics and students. Boyalik emphasized the importance of alternative methods in teaching and extracurricular activities, noting that effective and engaging strategies are essential for an active learning process. Highlighting that Türkiye has nearly one million teachers and 11 million students, he explained with examples how a single teacher even through a single activity can make a meaningful difference.

title: Turkey and Morocco’s Foreign Policies Discussed at IZU
content: Turkey Morocco’s Foreign Policies Discussed at IZU 03.12.2025 The “Turkey Foreign Policy” program, featuring Ambassador Ömer Faruk Dogan Foreign Trade Economic Diplomacy Expert Prof. Dr. Amine Laghidi as speakers, was held in Seminar Room 2 of Historical Building. The program was attended by academics students. Speaking at event, Ambassador Dogan introduced Turkey’s regional global foreign policy vision within framework of concepts of “Century of Türkiye” “New Türkiye Vision.” Emphasizing that Turkey is an important element of stability in its region, Dogan stated that country’s foreign policy is conducted on basis of benevolence, peace, broad international cooperation. Prof. Dr. Amine Laghidi noted that Kingdom of Morocco pursues a policy in Africa that is respectful of humanity, inclusive, focused on sustainable stability. Highlighting Morocco’s key role in maintaining regional stability, Laghidi emphasized importance of solidarity for sustainable development product safety.

title: “Post War Reconstruction of Syria Symposium” Held at IZU
content: “Post War Reconstruction of Syria Symposium” Held at IZU 01.12.2025 The “Post-War Reconstruction of Syria Symposium” was held at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. Organized in collaboration with Middle East Foundation, Turkish Journal of International Development, Omran Strategic Research Center, IZU, supported by Presidency for Turks Abroad Related Communities (YTB) TIKA, symposium addressed Syria’s reconstruction process in fields of education, economy, human capital, law. The symposium took place in Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall at IZU was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Erhan Içener, Omran Strategic Research Center Executive Board Member Ammar Kahf, Middle East Foundation Board Chair Prof. Mesut Özcan, as well as academics NGO representatives from Turkey abroad. ACAR: NATIONAL AND INTERNATIONAL SUPPORT IS CRUCIAL In his opening speech, IZU Rector Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar recalled that millions of Syrians were displaced tens of thousands lost their lives during 14-year conflict. He emphasized importance of justice, return of confiscated rights, access to education healthcare in reconstruction process. Acar stated, “Establishing political unity is also very important. The international order represented by UN has collapsed. At this stage, national international support is critical. The failure of Arab countries to fulfill their responsibilities has been a great disappointment. If we do not increase our cooperation, what happened in Syria yesterday could happen to us tomorrow.” KAHF: MORE THAN 50,000 SYRIAN UNIVERSITY STUDENTS IN TURKEY Ammar Kahf, Executive Director of Omran Strategic Research Center, highlighted serious lack of knowledge vision in Syria’s reconstruction. He said, “It is crucial to establish healthy relations with neighboring countries. We are aware of significance of Turkey opening its doors to millions of Syrians. Academic studies will shed light on reconstruction efforts. During war, Syrian academia declined, but millions who had to leave continued working elsewhere. Currently, Turkey hosts over 50,000 Syrian students at undergraduate graduate levels.” ÖZCAN: PROBLEMS CONTINUE IN SYRIA Prof. Mesut Özcan, Chair of Middle East Foundation, emphasized importance of holding symposium one year after Syrian revolution on December 8, 2024. He stated, “Significant change has started in Syria, but challenges questions persist. On first anniversary of revolution, we gathered to discuss what needs to be done to overcome them,” addressing difficulties of rebuilding cities, education, healthcare, other sectors. ÇELIK: RECONSTRUCTION HAS BEGUN RAPIDLY The first session, titled “Rebuilding Human Capital in New Era,” focused on Syria’s education system during reconstruction period. Prof. Zafer Çelik from Yildirim Beyazit University noted that a rapid reconstruction process began after December 8. He highlighted ongoing efforts to restructure curriculum, address teacher shortages, improve access to education. Çelik added, “Many projects are either partially implemented or in planning stages with Western countries, Arab world, Turkey. International organizations must honor their commitments to improve infrastructure make education system functional.” ATABAS: HUMAN RESOURCES NEEDED FOR SYRIA ARE IN THE DIASPORA Hacer Atabas from Africa Foundation emphasized role of universities in rebuilding state, restructuring socio-economic policies, ensuring social cohesion for war-affected population, establishing political stability. She noted that 14-year conflict disrupted academic institutions caused a significant brain drain. Atabas said, “The war destroyed education system. The human resources needed for reconstruction are in diaspora. Hundreds of thousands of Syrians continued their education abroad. If diaspora’s human capital returns, much of workforce need can be met.” DEMIR: ACADEMIC FREEDOM MUST BE GUARANTEED Sociologist Ayyüce Sahin Demir highlighted that higher education in Syria began in 1903 with Ottoman-established medical faculty in Damascus that by 2011, 22 universities were operational nationwide. Despite extensive destruction caused by war, higher education resumed rapidly after December 8. Demir emphasized importance of returning diaspora academics, stating conditions needed: “Academic freedom must be guaranteed. Physical infrastructure should be strengthened, solutions should be developed for housing needs of students academics.” MERT: ACADEMICS READY TO SUPPORT Mehmet Ali Mert from Recep Tayyip Erdogan University, final speaker of session, discussed role of diaspora academics in reconstruction. He emphasized that political stability is a prerequisite for their return: “State policies are important. Our discussions show that academics living abroad are ready to contribute. In addition to returning, intermediate solutions should also be considered.” The first day of symposium continued with sessions on “National International Dynamics in Reconstruction,” “Reconciling Syrian Society,” “Economic Management Reform,” “Building Economic Infrastructure.” On second day, topics included “Strengthening Private Sector,” “Financing Reconstruction,” “Building Security Structures,” “Compensating Losses Restoring Heritage,” “The Role of Syrian Refugees Diaspora,” “Transitional Justice Legal Reconstruction.”

title: Ismail Hakki Bursevi on the 300th Anniversary of His Passing
content: Ismail Hakki Bursevi on 300th Anniversary of His Passing 03.12.2025 The Great Islamic thinker, Celveti sheikh, commentator, poet Ismail Hakki Bursevi is being commemorated on 300th anniversary of his passing. At panel held at IZU, Mahmut Erol Kiliç Assoc. Ahmet Murat Özel discussed Bursevi's scholarly, mystical, literary legacy. The panel titled "Ismail Hakki Bursevi on 300th Anniversary of His Passing", organized by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Sufi Studies Research Center (ISTAM), took place at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. Moderated by Hasan Kamil Yilmaz, Dean of IZU Faculty of Islamic Sciences, panel hosted IRCICA Director General Mahmut Erol Kiliç Istanbul Medeniyet University faculty member Assoc. Ahmet Murat Özel. ACAR: "BURSEVI IS A GUIDING NORTH STAR" Speaking at opening of panel, IZU Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar described Ismail Hakki Bursevi as "a figure who guides us like a North Star in Today's darkness." Acar said: "Ismail Hakki Bursevi represents profile of what ancients called 'double-winged'. He was well-versed in rational sciences while also being one of leading figures in spiritual sciences. Recognizing such figures is an expression of respect for spiritual heritage they left behind." YILMAZ: ONE OF THE MOST PROLIFIC OTTOMAN SCHOLARS Hasan Kamil Yilmaz, Dean of IZU Faculty of Islamic Sciences, emphasized Bursevi was among most prolific Ottoman authors. Calling him "a man of balance a profound Sufi," Yilmaz noted Bursevî masterfully used both Arabic Turkish, his tafsir Ruh al-Bayan is considered a significant work among Ottoman scholars. KILIÇ: “SUFISM LIES AT THE FOUNDATION OF THE SCIENCES” Mahmut Erol Kiliç began by reminding audience Sufism was traditionally considered foundation of all sciences in pre-modern world. He pointed out modern era detached itself from this princible, leading to claim each discipline stands on its own. Addressing Bursevi's detailed treatment of concept of inner/esoteric politics, Kiliç said this perspective offered a Sufi conceptual framework for understanding Ottoman state structure. He added Bursevî’s writings across different fields should not be seen as scattered: “In their world of meaning, all sciences serve same purpose. A life or human being without meaning is unimaginable. Spiritual sciences give meaning to concepts. What makes us who we are is this meaning. The role of prophets saints is to breathe meaning back into souls remind us of true essence of religions risk remaining mere rituals.” ÖZEL: “BURSEVÎ IS A RIPE FRUIT OF THE OTTOMAN EMPIRE” Discussing Bursevî’s poetic dimension, Assoc. Ahmet Murat Özel stated Bursevî, as a scholar, commentator, Sufi, traveler, poet, left behind a rich corpus. “Born in mid-1600s, during Ottoman Empire’s stagnation period, Bursevî is a ripe fruit of Ottoman intellectual tradition,” he said. Özel emphasized themes of love spiritual guidance stand out in his poetry, everyday details within his verses offer valuable biographical insights. The program concluded with a musical performance in which ilahis composed from Bursevî’s poems were sung by Mehmet Öncel, Chair of IZU Music Education Department.

title: Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Turkish Education and Teaching Application and Research Center Regulation
content: Teaching Application Research Center Regulation ISTANBUL SABAHATTIN ZAIM UNIVERSITY TURKISH EDUCATION AND TEACHING APPLICATION AND RESEARCH CENTRE REGULATION SECTION ONE Aim, Scope, Basis Definitions Aim 1 - This Regulation has been prepared in order to regulate principles procedures regarding fields of activity, administrative bodies their duties working principles of Teaching Application Research Centre. Scope 2 – (2) This Regulation comprises provisions regarding Teaching Application Research Centre’s administrative bodies, their duties working principles. Basis 3 – (3) This Regulation is based on sub clause (2) of clause (d) of first paragraph of article 7 article 14 of Higher Law dated 4/11/1981 numbered 2547. Definitions 4 – following terms in Regulation have meanings ascribed to them herein: a) Centre (IZUTEM): Teaching Application Research Centre, b) Director: of Teaching Application Research Centre, c) Rector: Rector of University, d) Senate: Senate of University, e) University: University, f) Board: of Teaching Application Research Centre. SECTION TWO Fields of Activity of IZUTEM Fields of Activity 5 – Fields of activity of IZUTEM as follows, a) To establish units at home abroad in order to promote Turkey culture. b) To open courses lecturers teachers assigned to teach at home abroad by relevant state institutions organizations; to have them do internship also to prepare introductory handbooks about country where they will work in. c) To carry out studies to improve language teaching methods applied in teaching departments of universities; to do research applications on language teaching methods. d) To perform comparative studies between foreign languages in order to make teaching more efficient; to develop relevant materials assessment-evaluation tools. e) To organize courses children living abroad. f) To cooperate universities other academic institutions that teach abroad or have relevant departments. g) To teach to students academicians coming as part of interuniversity cooperation protocols. h) To carry out teaching, research, application, publication activities jointly relevant units of universities other state institutions organisations in framework of bilateral agreements made by Turkey. i) To prepare programmes develop methods; to cooperate various institutions organisation at home abroad concerning this matter. j) To issue a certificate in accordance Common European Language Criteria (CELC). k) To teach to foreign students coming as part of European Union projects. l) To teach to foreign students want to study at university, to academicians employees. m) To coordinate language teaching application programs senior students graduate of teaching Language Literature departments of universities. n) To teach to groups individuals within frame of protocols institutions. SECTION THREE Bodies Bodies 6 – administrative bodies of centre stated below. Director. Board. (7) – is appointed by rector from among faculty of relevant departments of three years. After completing term of office can be recommissioned. In case of Director’s absence, vice-director represents director. If is absent more than six months, duty of is automatically terminated. Rector assigns a vice-director among faculty of upon suggestion of Director. Duties of 8 – duties of as follows: a) To represent Centre. b) To moderate Board. c) To ensure that IZUTEM’s activities conducted regularly, to monitor control them. d) To submit decisions taken by to Rector’s approval. e) To delegate duties required by IZUTEM’s works. f) To present report on general situation functioning of IZUTEM at end of every academic year to Rector after taking opinion of Board. 9 – consists of a total of five including Director, vice-director, three to be appointed by Rector from among relevant faculty of University. (2) term of office of of is three years. complete their terms of duty may be recommissioned. new assigned to complete remaining time of duty of leave before expiry date or assigned outside more than three months. meets absolute majority upon invitation of decisions taken by majority vote. Duties of 10 – duties of as follow: a) To take decisions in regards to activities administration of IZUTEM. b) To examine suggestions of on issues regarding determination appointment of personnel to be assigned in IZUTEM to decide its proposal to Rectorate. c) To discuss report that is to be prepared by at end of every activity year to prepare working schedule next year. d)To specify number of students to be admitted to IZUTEM every year. e) To comment on issues regarding research, publication teaching. SECTION FOUR Student Admission Registration Principles Student Admission 11 – To IZUTEM; those applied to from abroad accepted decision of Board; students academicians come under interuniversity cooperation protocols; foreign students coming to University; foreigners at home abroad; children of families living abroad other people want to learn can register as a student. course attendee has registered courses accepts all applications conditions in IZUTEM. Registration 12 – conditions of applications, registration dates, documents required registration principles to be followed determined announced by IZUTEM. Tuition Fee 13 – In IZUTEM, education is paid is determined annually. Tuition fees per year and/or per course/credit determined each year by submitted to Rector’s approval; announced after approval, together form of payment. SECTION FIVE Teaching Principles Modules, execution of courses certification 14 – Courses at IZUTEM carried out according to module system. Module levels determined in accordance Common European Language Criteria (CELC). (2) Modules that carried out as weekdays and/or weekends, in levels of Basic Language Usage (A1, A2), Independent Language Usage (B1, B2), Advanced Language Usage (C1) each module is at least 140 (a hundred forty) hours. (3) compulsory attendance to courses is 80% (eighty percent). Students incomplete course attendance not taken to final exams deemed to have failed that module. No refund will be made days hours that not attended during course. (4) A certificate is given to those have successfully completed modules. Foreign students’ starting programs 15 – Students enrolled in any undergraduate or graduate program of our have Independent Language Usage (B2) Certificate of IZUTEM start department where they registered. Success evaluation 16 – assessment of student success is done according to attendance, studies in semester, results of quizzes final exams. evaluation is based on listening, reading, dialogue, verbal expression written expression. weighted grades of these determined by decision of announced to students. SECTION SIX Miscellaneous Final Provisions Cases no provision 17 – In cases where there no provisions in this Regulation, other relevant legislation provisions decisions of Senate applied. repealed regulation 18 – Regulation of Teaching Application Research Centre, published in Official Gazette dated 27/11/2013 numbered 28834 has been abolished. Validity 19 – This Regulation becomes valid on date of its publication. Execution 20 – This Regulation is executed by Rector of University. Note: Whenever there is any conflict between any provision of this Regulations, its version is taken into account.

title: Staff Mobility
content: Erasmus+ Staff Mobility Activities STAFF From ABROAD Are you interested in teaching or training at IZU? View pages for incoming staff STAFF From IZU Are you interested in teaching or training abroad? View pages for outgoing staff General Information Staff Mobility for Teaching Erasmus+ supports teaching periods at higher education institutions (HEIs) abroad. If you work in higher education, you can spend time teaching at an institution in an Erasmus+ Programme or Partner country. Teaching opportunites are also available to staff working in Programme country organisations. Examples of such organisations include businesses, public bodies, social partners, research institutes. Staff Mobility for Training Erasmus+ supports training periods for staff working in higher education institutions. With Erasmus+, you can train at a higher education institution in an Erasmus+ Programme or Partner country or at an organisation outside the sector in a Programme country. Examples of such organisations include businesses, public bodies, social partners, research institutes and non-governmental organisations.

title: Education in Turkey
content: Education in It is highly recommended foreigners wish to reasons, should overview related our country’s visa procedures in advance before arrival. Visa procedure touristic business purposed visits to a country differ in relative to work visits. Also Turkey’s visa policies change country to country according to reciprocity principle. Some applicants be exempted getting a visa to if it is touristic or business purposes. Others obtain touristic Visa through electronic Visa. All applicants want to obtain a visa working purposes to apply to our Representations abroad. According to countries, an note Turkey’s visa regime is available in following address: www.mfa.gov.tr Foreigners wish to enter to carry a visa, a visa exemption, date of resident permit duration ends. They to request a passport or documentations shows a passport is valid atleast 60 days. Exampe: A Foreign visitor owns a visa 90 days resident permit duration, they to 150 days (90 days + 60 days/ 5 month) valid passport or a travel document at time of entrance in Turkey. Passports must a validity of 6 months atleast expiration date of visa. They 90 days to do so. visa information, please Ministry of Foreign Affairs’ official website: http://www.mfa.gov.tr/sub.en.mfa?cc4e437c-6769-4d79-9017-10b63c651224 regarding residence permit, please National Police’s official website: http://www.egm.gov.tr/EN/Pages/Residence-Permit-Procedures.aspx Please keep in mind university assist residence permit procedures. do to do it alone, help is there. Many Universities in provide campus accommodation to wish to stay on campus or in state dormitories. Meals generally included in fee but dormitories canteens where eat breakfast meals a nominal, relatively low cost. All fees fixed in Liras. Regardless of nationality, must pay same amount as students. In addition, most dormitories segregated by sex. It is advised bring enough Money as cash as credit card so it be easily converted to Lira. Credit cards be accepted. In Turkey, processes related to accommodation in state dormitories carried out by General Directorate of Higher Education Credit Hostels Institution. detailed information, please www.kyk.gov.tr web page. If looking a room share, find vacancies posted in hostels, language schools, or similiar areas regarding accommodation. In addition to sources, a chance to find an apartment in university’s campus. All recieving in take advantage of “general insurance” coverage in Turkey. Students "general insurance", use state hospitals without any fee, use private hospitals by paying a moderate fee. To benefit General Health Insurance System in Turkey, go to school’s Student Office to get information. Then necessary documents, go to Social Security Institution to apply. When complete application, pay general insurance fee which is determined on an annual basis within 1 month date of Social Security records. If don't apply general insurance system within given time period (3 months) cannot benefit general insurance services of State. But, if an insurance they use it or get benefit private insurances in Turkey. Some offer special insurance policy students. Please bear in mind is known providing high quality services at low costs. reason patients neighbouring countries as well as developed western countries prefer to come to to get healthier cheaper treatments surgery, dental, eye examinations. Students In order to face any problems until they benefit healt insurance in Turkey, we recommend them to go checkups, dentists.We recommend to bring along glasses, a spare pair of contact lenses optical prescription them. Lastly bringing along a first-aid kit be really useful. is home to world’s earliest settlements numerous civilizations ranging smallest of communities to greatest of empires. is cradle of cultures civilizations connecting Europe Asia capital of civilizations reined lands of Anatolia centuries. Since beginning of history, Anatolia, well known as one of earliest settlements, continued to flourish migration of various tribes, accumulated a large cultural heritage through a line of succeeding empires civilizations. Many empires ranging Sumerians to Hittites, Lydians to Byzantines Seljuks to Ottomans once thrived expired within borders of Turkey. To get holiday cities of to decide which cities to in Turkey, please official tourism portal of http://www.goturkeytourism.com Mediterranean Aegean climates a cool moist winter season, a warm rainless summer season. climate in Aegean region of is great throughout year, even during winter. weather in eastern region of Turkey, bordering Georgia Iraq, hot summers snowy icy winters. Turkey’s climate weather along coast of Black Sea is a continental climate. In Turkey, humidity levels be quite high. During winter, especially in months of December January, weather is cold temperatures drop below zero. weather in Turkey’s capital, Ankara, as well as central Anatolian region is cold in winters hot dry during summer. Turkey’s central climate is known as a mixture between a continental climate a steppe climate. To get on Turkey’s weather please State Meteorological Service’s official website: http://www.mgm.gov.tr/ homeland to numerous communities civilizations beginning of history of mankind Anatolia is hosting magnificent Culture hundreds of years. Due to geographical continents connects Europe Asia, culture evolving influence of civilizations. Anatolia cradle of civilizations throughout history effects of cultures reflect cuisine social life. If curious about; customs, traditions, folk dances, theatre, cinema, fine arts, literature, traditional modern music,please official tourism portal of http://www.goturkeytourism.com One of best things culture is variety of its cuisine! find only food, but delicious samples of food western eastern kitchen cuisine. Eating habits shaped according to prevalent cultural, geographical, ecological, economic characteristics is shaped according to historical duration. In summary, variety of products offered by lands of Asia Anatolia, interaction numerous cultures over a long historical process, new tastes developed in palace kitchens of Seljuk Ottoman empires played a part in shaping our new culinary culture, which would very much enjoy. traditional Ottoman cuisine more, please official website of Ministry of Culture Tourism: http://www.kultur.gov.tr/EN,35306/culinary-culture.html These afew popular native foods in Turkey: 1- Kebap 2- Simit 3- Yogurt –Ayran 4- Kahvalti (Balli-Kaymakli) 5- Lokum 6- Baklava 7- Findik Fistik (Kuruyemisler) 8- Meyveler 9- Pide 10- Lahmacun 11- Tursu 12- Maras Dondurmasi 13- Manti 14- Dolma – Sarma 15- Kuru Fasulye 16- Kestane Sekeri / Kebab 17- Tavukgögsü Tatlisi 18- Türk Kahvesi – Lokumu 19- Börek 20- Sahlep (Vahsi orkide köklerinden kremali – tarçinli mucize içecek) 21- Asure 22- Boza monetary unit is Lira (TL). Traveler’s checks favored nor accepted in Turkey. ATMs be found in even smallest towns. Most accept credit cards or bank cards (a strip of logos is usually displayed above ATM). Almost ATMs a language key to enable to read instructions in English. Most commercial establishments accept credit cards such as Visa, Master Card American Express. banking systems highly developed, easily find a bank ATM anywhere. make payments at any ATM’s by using credit cards. open an account on name, to get an ATM card a resident document. value-added tax, is known as KDV, is 18%. Value-added tax is nearly always included in quoted prices. Certain shops deduct tax, therefore to ask whether any deductions be made. electrical current in is 220 volts as Alternating current (AC) 50 hertz cycles alternating current (AC); wall outlets take Continental-type plugs, two or three round prongs. Jan 1: First Day of New Year Apr 23: National Sovereignty Children's Day (anniversary of establishment of Grand National Assembly in 1920) May 1: Labour Solidarity Day (since 2009) May 19: Atatürk Commemoration Youth & Sports Day (the arrival of Atatürk in Samsun in 1919, beginning of War of Independence) Aug 30: Victory Day (victory over invading forces in 1922). Oct 29: Republic Day (anniversary of declaration of Republic in 1923). Ramazan Bayrami: Three-day festival is celebrated end of fast of Ramadan month. Also known as "Seker (sweets) Bayrami" because it's customary to offer candies to family members friends come to visit. Kurban Bayrami: Four-day festival where sheeps or cows sacrificed meat distributed to poor. dates of religious festivals change according to Islamic calendar thus occur 10-11 days earlier each year. (This is due to 10 days 21 hrs difference between Gregorian Lunar calendars). According to this; Standard time zone: UTC/GMT + 3 hours To make a long-distance call (any call outside of city code), to dial long-distance prefix (0) before seven-digit local phone number. Below area codes Turkey´s major cities: Adana 322 Ankara 312 Antalya 242 Bursa 224 Istanbul (Asian side) 216 Istanbul (European side) 212 Izmir 232 Each mobile provider assigned a range of area codes (or mobile prefixes). Turkcell 530-539, Vodafone 541-549, Turk Telekom 502-506 Important Note: exactly 120 days of duration to use mobile phones bring along you. Before duration ends, to go to a GSM Operator passport in order to register mobile device. operation cost 140TL, or 38 $. If purchase device do to do process. Useful telephone service numbers: 155 Police 112 Ambulance 110 Firefighter 170 Tourism Info Planning travel to is exciting, however before going to Turkey, find tips below useful: Documents to bring along you: passport, a valid visa; travel documentations, tickets a acceptance letter university. (The address of new university, plus telephone number of person related be helpful to you). Health travel insurance: Firstly, get advice university – they offer a special insurance policy students. Asking insurance companies be helpful. vaccinations health-related considerations, check out our section. Do forget most airlines charge excess baggage. Temperatures in vary season to season region to region. Turkey’s weather, check our Weather section. Bridge to World reasons to come to Turkey! Each outskirt its own spiritual geographical features attracts people around world. example, Istanbul is only city in world connects two continents, Asia Europe. city, which is located in both Asia Europe, capital city civilizations thousands of years throughout its history. is than a bridge between two continents. Actually, is a country in which East West meet in a much broader sense. successful in blending two cultures being home to cultural historical richness legacy thousands of years. A Lot of Scholarship Opportunities chance to study abroad is a dream students, especially in today’s global era. According to UNESCO, 3,6 million were enrolled in higher abroad in 2010, number is increasing every year. In challenging competitive environment, there a wide variety of scholarship opportunities in available you. Many of scholarships provided by republic of State. information, please http://www.turkiyeburslari.gov.tr/index.php/en/turkiye-burslari/burs-programlari There scholarship processes in private universities. (%100 - %75 - %50 Scholarships An Ideal Place Living experience of studying abroad is than just attending a school in a foreign country. So, deciding where to study abroad, should first ask yourself where want to live. With its cosmopolitan cities small towns, offers a variety of cultural experiences to students. Bringing cultures, religions, people, lifestyles definitely cuisines together. served as a gate to Western World Eastern cultures to Eastern World Western societies. Weather is another important consideration to take deciding to study abroad. is one of few countries experience enjoy four seasons thanks to its geographical position climate. Being surrounded by three seas, is an ideal destination where see lots of sun during summer enough snow to do skiing in winter. Costs of Living Studying costs of studying living abroad is another factor to take into consideration while making decision studying abroad. Compared to student destinations, offers significantly lower annual tuition fees much lower living expenses. According to Mercer’s 2013 Global cost of living rankings, Turkey’s metropolitan cities in very bottom row of most expensive cities list. A Door to Your Dream Job One of main questions after graduation is “what happen after this?”International graduate a lot of options. example choose to continue by doing a master’s degree, return home a widely accepted diploma ease them to find a job either in or out of Turkey. In addition, able to work part-time on campus during education. is progressing towards being one of leading economies of world its developing growing economy. number of Global brands doing business in working together brands is increasing. As of June 2013, number of companies operating in is 34,000, 50% of companies European Union countries. As is a favorite country investors, chance to work in one of companies. witness classmates or friends achieve significant success in careers in even in world. So, use contacts to open doors to new opportunities yourself. Quality of Education In last 10 years, higher system developed significantly both in terms of quality quantity. now offer a rich field of choices students. quality of system reflected ranking lists. According to Times Higher Education BRICS & Emerging Economies Rankings 2014, 7 entered in top 100 university list, 3 of them in top 10. In addition, 9 took place among top 800 world ranked by Quacquarelli Symonds (QS) in UK. 19 took place among top 1000 world ranked by METU Informatics Institute. offer high quality studies a integral part of European space. There is an increasing number of offering programs taught in English, include medicine, engineering, humanities, business, finance. is part of Bologna Process since 2001 a member of platforms. is important in two ways. First being continue in countries ECTS (European Credit Transfer System) second they get diplomas recognized by countries. University – Industry Cooperation Regarding link between university-industry, starting year 2000, techno-parks technology development centers established within university campuses. Law on Technology Development Centers, which entered into force in 2001, draws framework legal status of institutions. As of 2014, there still 32 technology development centers operating an additional 11 under construction in universities. Associate Degree qualifications included in higher system. Within degree, offer two-year programs, of which vocational oriented at end of two year study period, graduates expected to be employed as intermediate staff in related sectors. curriculum design of programs is done in such a way gives student opportunity to do internships in firms of related sectors. Language In Turkey, most common languages used in higher institutions are; Turkish, English, Arabic. There higher institutions where is taught in German or French. However, if applied to an academic program is taught in Turkish, expected to learn Turkish. is one of most common spoken languages in World. those International want to enhance Turkish, is an ideal place studying in English to learn aswell. There some alternative ways to do so: Language Teaching Research Center at respective university (TÖMER) Language Center at respective university Yunus Emre Institute Cultural Centers, please visit: http://www.yee.org.tr/en/ Private Language Schools Student Clubs Hello Merhaba mehr-hah-bah Good morning Günaydin gew-naye-dun Good evening Iyi aksamlar ee ahk-shahm-lahr Good-bye (said by person leaving) Hosçakal Hosh cha kal Good-bye (said by person staying) Güle güle guele guele How you? Nasilsin? nah-suhl-suhn What’s up / How’re doing? (Informal) Naber? na berr I am fine Iyiyim ee-yeem Thank Tesekkür ederim. te-shek-kewr-lehr You’re welcome Birsey degil. (in response to “Thank you”) beer-shey deh-eel Please Lütfen. lewt-fehn I don’t understand Anlamadim an-la-ma-duhm Pleased to meet Memnum oldum mem-noon oll-doom I am sorry Pardon puhr-dohn Excuse me Pardon puhr-dohn Yes Evet. eh-vet No Hayir. hah-yuhr Bon Appetite! Afiyet olsun! af-ee-yeht ul-sun What? Ne? neh Help! Imdat! im-duht When? Ne zaman? neh-zah-mah One bir beer Two iki ee-kee Three üç ewch Four dört dirt Five bes besh Six alti ahl-tuh Seven yedi yeh-dee Eight sekiz seh-keez Nine dokuz doh-kooz Ten on ohn Literal Translation 1. Hos geldiniz ”Your arrival is lovely/welcome” Said as a greeting to visitors to make them feel at home. 2. Seytan tüyü “The devil’s feather” What seems like an insult is actually a compliment used to describe a mysterious, devilish charm. 3. Tas atti da kolu mu yoruldu? “Did he throw a stone so his arm got tired?” Said to berate who’s being lazy. 4. Bir yastikta kocasinlar they grow old one pillow” By they mean to tell couples to never go to bed angry, is said to newlyweds traditionally share a single, long pillow in a marital bed. 5. Etekleri zil çaliyor “The bells on hems ringing” I recently learned evocative saying used to describe is very excited. 6. Pabucu dama atildi “His shoe thrown onto roof” Often said of a father a new baby monopolises mother’s attentions, or by anyone passed over in favour of another. 7. Balik etli “Fish fleshed” men usually like women a bit of meat on bones, is a lovely compliment to curvy girls. 8. Havadan sudan “Of air water” Turks love to chat, often at length, nothing in particular. is a great way of describing idle chatter — a lot like Irish craic. 9. Elini sallasa ellisi “If he waves his hand, fifty come” A great way to comfort a friend after a breakup, it means there plenty of fish in sea. 10. Cigerimin kösesi “The corner of my liver” anatomic description is actually used to describe is very precious to you. might hear it featured as a lyric in love songs. 11. Armut pis agzima düs pear be cooked on tree fall into my mouth” describes a person doesn’t like to work, to whom everything comes ready done — or falls literally in lap. 12. Iyi ki dogdun “It’s good were born” Happy Birthday. I think expression is a lot meaningful than English version, I even use literal English translation on my non-Turkish friends’ birthdays. 13. Bosver “Give empty” Perhaps my favorite saying, Bosver means “let it go,” a la Frozen. 14. El elden üstündür “A hand is superior to another person’s hand” Said to show there’s always do a better job. 15. Batti balik yan gider “A sunken fish goes sideways” one’s pretty hard to parse, but it means worst-case scenario already happened (the fish sunk), so might as well stop worrying some fun! 16. Ates almaya gelmek “Coming over just a light” cheekily accuse a visitor of want them to stay a while longer. 17. Cami yikilmis ama mihrab yerinde ”The mosque is a ruin but mihrap is standing” One of my personal favorites, phrase describes an older woman retains her charms. It’s like saying a church is in ruins but pulpit is standing. Basically, it’s opposite of “mutton dressed as lamb.” 18. Geçmis olsun it be past” Said is sick or had a bad experience. I love how it recognizes someone’s pain, but expresses hope it soon be behind them. 19. Nazar degmesin be touched by evil eye” Said after giving a compliment, particularly to a child. Although I’m a pretty rational person, I do believe in power of evil eye use saying a lot. 20. Hayirli olsun it be auspicious” Said whenever a new undertaking, such as a new job. 21. Allah anali babali büyütsün God let him/her grow up both a mom dad” Perhaps most meaningful thing could wish a newborn child. 22. Kolay gelsin ”May it be easy you” is a great way to acknowledge another person’s labors, even a stranger on street, a difficult task at hand. 23. Çok yasa live long” “bless you,” çok yasa is said after sneezes. sneezer then replies sende gör (may see my life) or hep beraber (may we years to live). 24. Kafayi üsüttü “They’ve caught a cold in head” Turks a congenital fear of catching a chill, which strike any part of body. If you’ve caught a cold in head, it means you’ve gone crazy. 25. Sihhatler olsun it bring health” Older generations grew up before hot running water in homes, only had baths once or twice a week, say come out of shower in case catch a chill (see above). 26. Basiniz sagolsun head stay healthy” A thoughtful way of wishing condolences they grieving loss of a loved one. 27. (Benim) ilk göz agrim “The first pain of my eye” is said to a first love or first child to avoid giving them evil eye (I used to feel very jealous my grandmother said to my brother, her oldest grandchild.) 28. Ellerine saglik “Health to hand” Said to created something beautiful hands, particularly to a cook or a mom after she’s cooked up a feast her family. 29. Bir musibet bin nasihatten iyidir “One bad experience is worth a thousand warnings” No explanation needed. 30. Güle güle gidin go laughing” is said to departing visitors to help take edge off sad goodbyes.

title: Equivalence Certificate
content: Certificate It certifies that taken is equivalent to in Turkey. You cannot enroll to a university in Turkey without of equivalence. Here is how obtain of equivalence. Here steps to obtain of equivalence: 1-Select place to apply which suits best: It is better easier to apply of while in country. You need find office of Educational Attaché in country. Please click here to see list of educational attaché in country. If country is on list, need to apply of as soon as come to Turkey. 2-Prepare required documents: Original of diploma transcripts of all years should be ready when apply of equivalence. Please do forget to get stamp on original documents. Because either Educational Attaché in country or Provincial Directorate of do accept documents, which not, verified by Ministry of Foreign Affairs. 3-Applying in Turkey Students may apply online at https://edenklik.meb.gov.tr/ . All of detailed information is available at https://edenklik.meb.gov.tr/ . Suggestion: It is recommended to choose PROVINCIAL: Istanbul, DISTRICT: Büyüksehir in selection of Center when applying online Certificate. 4-Take original back Provincial Directorate of Education: After evaluate application of equivalence, either will issue of or will be able to issue it. If issue certificate, should bring all required to Student Affairs to complete registration. Important Notes: If there is any Educational Attache in home country or if taken of equivalence, yet should apply as soon as arrive to Turkey. Bear in mind that application process of may take a long time. The students who did submit their valid residence permit at time of application will most probably get temporary of equivalence. Then, if bring residence permit, their permanent of will be issued. What is Certificate? A report taken primary secondary institutions abroad. Transcript, certificate, schools examined to find out equivalent in Turkey such as area, department, branch. (Ministry of Regulation) Where do I need to apply Certificate? processing; provincial national directorates in Turkey, counselors attache offices abroad. Applications made to these institutions. (Ministry of Regulation) What required processing? For those who applied certificate, originals photocopies of following shall be attached to Application Form. (Ek-1 Form) a) Documents last primary school year received schools attended, of separation if there is a learning certificate. b) Documents last secondary school year received schools attended, of separation if there is a learning certificate. c) Secondary school completion certificate, of study last academic year received institution of study abroad, diploma (original "Apostille" certified diploma). d) Translation made by sworn translators of passport used during study abroad, or entry exit dates pages of passport. e) In case that there is no field instructor or specialist in institution to perform process, Turkish translations made by sworn translators of foreign accepted. (Ministry of Regulation) * Translation of required in these languages: English, German, Arabic, Persian, Russian, Bulgarian, Bosnian Romanian. Do I to a valid residence permit or study visa foreigners? Yes. (MEB. TTKB. No. 2236435 dated 28/08/2013) What do I need to do to get Certificate? Complete all needed application. Documents’ originals photocopies should be put in a transparent file. The application form is completed signed. The applications shall be made to Higher Overseas Branch (Office 1) by person or a legal guardian on request Certificate. After files examined, delivered to "Document Registration" unit with special file (transparent cover) necessary processing. At end of necessary examinations made by relevant experts, equivalents of those deemed appropriate prepared. (Approximately 14 days) The originals of handed over at application stage submitted to applicant or his legal representative in special file (transparent cover) together with Certificate. Certificate Application Form Please click here to download application form. Where is Istanbul Provincial Directorate of Education? Address : Binbirdirek M. Imran Öktem, Eski Toptasi Caddesi Eski Adliye Binasi D: 1, 34122 Sultanahmet / Fatih / Istanbul Working hours: Monday 08: 30-17: 00 Tuesday 08: 30-17: 00 Wednesday 08: 30-17: 00 Thursday 08: 30-17: 00 Friday 08: 30-17: 00 Saturday Closed Sunday Closed Phone: (0212) 455 04 00 Map : I'm coming schools opened abroad by Republic of Turkey Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus. Do I to get an Certificate? The students coming schools opened abroad by Republic of Turkey Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus subjected to this process. These students will be enrolled in same kind of school according to of attestation, diploma other educational documents, files requested school. Registration admission to schools that receive students with a central exam aptitude test in our country carried out according to provisions of special regulations of schools. (Ministry of Regulation) I completed my via internet or distance system. Can I get an certificate? For electronic education/distance methods operations performed. (Ministry of Regulation) I gained right to continue to a higher-level class where I had in a country/institution. Would there be a drawback to process? Those who earned right to attend a higher by completing school level in country where arrive in a shorter period of time, their rights to attend a higher level will be protected. (Ministry of Regulation) How to confirm Certificate? The Equivalency Documents can be confirmed by going through following steps website " evraksorgu.meb.gov.tr ". - Enter security code. - Enter document number. (The last 7 digits in heading 'Number' at top of Certificate) - Enter document code. (Located at bottom of Certificate) - Click on Send button.

title: Mobility for Tranieeships
content: Mobility Tranieeships Erasmus + Traineeship Mobility enables student enrolled in a higher education institution to undertake an internship in a company located in one of Erasmus + Program Countries. Traineeship activity is that student obtains practical work experience in field of education. In this context, student is expected to obtain professional training / or working experience in institution where internship is made. Erasmus+ Program Countries United Kingdom, Denmark, The Netherlands, Ireland, Sweden, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Czech Republic, Finland, France, Iceland, Italy, Cyprus, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Norway, Poland, Romania, Turkey *, Greece, Germany , Spain, Latvia, Macedonia, Malta, Portugal, Slovak Republic, Estonia, Croatia, Lithuania, Serbia, Slovenia. The grant amounts provided countries are included in application announcements published by Erasmus Institution Coordinatorship. Turkey is a country in Erasmus + program. However, participants selected from our university can not perform their mobility in Turkey. Eligible Institutions / Organizations An appropriate enterprise is an enterprise that engages in all kinds of economic activities,no matter their size, legal status economic sector in which they operate, all kinds of private or public institutions / organizations. A Higher Education Institiution with ECHE (if internship is going to be done at a higher education institution, a bilateral agreement is not a must) Or Any public institution or private institution / organization operating in labor market or in fields of education, training youth. Institutions / organizations internship may be one of following. Small, medium or large private or public institutions or organizations (including social initiatives) A public institution at local, regional or national level A social partner or other representative of business life, including chambers of commerce, trades / professional associations trade unions Research institute Foundation School / institute / training center (including at any level, from pre-primary education to institution of high school post-secondary education (including vocational training adult education) Nonprofit organization, association, NGO Institutions / organizations providing career guidance, professional advice information services Ineligible Institutions/Organizations The following organizations are not eligible higher education internship under Erasmus +: European institutions EU agencies (see https://europa.eu/european-union/about-eu/institutions-bodies_en ) National Agency implementation of EU programs, etc. organizations. Ineligible Mobilities Internship activities academic study cannot be carried out. Internship activity cannot be used to carry out studies such as research assignments, analysis studies on academic studies within scope of a specific curriculum. Students' studies, scientific research projects to complete or support their own scientific studies are not accepted as internship activities. Internship is not work of students who are under supervision of a consultant, who are in thesis period, but who are not covered by internship. Minimum Maximum Durations The duration of activity student traineeship mobility by European Commission is determined as at least 2 months maximum 12 months. However, in order participants to benefit from Erasmus + internship mobility at our university, activity period to be granted is limited to a minimum of 60 days maximum 65 days. The mobility period must be carried out continuously. During mobility, if business is closed because of holidays, it means your mobility gets interrupted holiday period.Grants will be paid period when business is closed, but closed time will not be added to activity period. In order to ensure minimum mobility time, dates of internship to be closed must be investigated in advance it is necessary to ensure that minimum period is ensured even after holiday period is removed. Weekend holidays are not vacation period to be deducted from activity period. In case of student traineeship mobility, when certificate of participation is observed to be active less than 2 months in participation certificate, activity is considered invalid no grant payment is made to beneficiary. The student who has been determined to perform less than 2 months in certificate of participation must return entire grant.

title: Mobility for Studies
content: Mobility for Studies Erasmus+ Student Mobility for Studies enables students enrolled in our university to complete a part of their education at higher education institution (which has an inter-institutional agreement with IZU) in Erasmus+ Program Countries. Erasmus+ Programme Countries United Kingdom, Denmark, Netherlands, Ireland, Sweden, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Czech Republic, Finland, France, Iceland, Italy, Cyprus, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Norway, Poland, Romania, Turkey*, Greece, Germany , Spain, Latvia, Macedonia, Malta, Portugal, Slovak Republic, Estonia, Croatia, Lithuania, Slovenia. The grant amounts provided by destination countries are included in application announcements published on university's website. Turkey is a country involved in Erasmus+ Program. However, participants selected from our university can not perform mobility in Turkey. Eligible Institutions / Organizations The institution where mobility will be performed must meet following 2 conditions. The institution in which mobility will be carried out should be a higher education institution which has ECHE. There should be an inter-institutional agreement including study field between higher education institution and our university. Please click here for Erasmus+ inter-institutional agreements. Minimum and Maximum Duration The duration of activity is limited by European Commission as at least 3 months and not more than 12 months. The duration of activity is limited by European Commission as at least 3 months and not more than 12 months. The mobility period must be performed continuously. In cases where mobility is stated as less than 3 months in participation certificate, activity is considered invalid and no grant payment is made to beneficiary. The student who has less than 3 months of participation in certificate of participation must return entire grant.

title: 2019-2020 Fall - ICM - Jordan - Middle East University (MEU)- Application for Incoming Student Mobility for Studies
content: 2019-2020 Fall - ICM - Jordan - Middle East University (MEU)- Application Incoming Student Mobility Studies Overall Assessment Result Pre Assessment Result FOR MIDDLE EAST UNIVERSITY (MEU) FACULTY OF BUSINESS STUDENTS 2019-2020 ACADEMIC YEAR, FALL SEMESTER ERASMUS + ICM MOBILITY WITH PARTNER COUNTRIES APPLICATION ANOUNCEMENT FOR STUDENT MOBILITIY FOR STUDIES Turkey– Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Contract Number: 2018-1-TR01-KA107-056256 Calendar 6 May 2019 Announcement of Application 28 May 2019 Beginning of online period 28 June 2019 End of online period 29 June 2019 Pre-assessment results 30 June – 7 July Reclamation period pre assessment results 25 July 2019 Announcement of Final evaluation 25-31 July 2019 Waiver dates (give up dates) Erasmus+, Partner Countries Student Mobility Studies Student Mobility Studies enables enrolled in our university to complete a part of their education at higher education institution (which has an inter-institutional agreement IZU) in Program Countries. Who can apply? Middle East University, Faculty of Business students. Minimum requirements In order to participate in activity, meet following minimum requirements: Full-Time Student of MEU Faculty of Business. To be a Full-Time Student Must be enrolled in an education program also there be enough courses which can register before graduation. To graduate, should have at least 1 semester credits (30 ECTS) to complete. Students who meet requirements of academic grade point average, The cumulative academic grade point average of undergraduate is at least 2.20 / 4.00. title: 2017-2018 Spring - Staff Mobility for Training Application Announcement
content: 2017-2018 Spring - Mobility Training Application Announcement 2017-2018 AKADEMIC YEAR SPRING SEMESTER (4 April - 31 May 2018) ERASMUS+ STAFF MOBILITY FOR TRAINING - APPLICATION ANNOUNCEMENT Project Number: 2016-1-TR01-KA103-028807 Calendar 01 February 2018 2017-2018 Academic Year Spring Semester Mobility Announcement 15 March 2018 2017-2018 Academic Year Spring Semester Mobility Application Start Date 31 March 2018 2017-2018 Academic Year Spring Semester Mobility Application End Date 31 March 2018 Announcement of election results 2-3 April 2018 Objections to election results waiving Dates 4 April 2018 Determination of noble, spare ungranted candidates announcement of placement results Possible updates on application calendar can be followed up by our web page of IZU Erasmus. Important note: Participation in be between 5.04.2018 to 31.05.2018. Mobility Training with Erasmus + Program Countries of program allows of higher education institutions to have a minimum of 2 days a maximum of 2 months in another program country. Full-time working at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University can receive in a Program country. How to Apply? Personnel want to apply fill in online application form at: Application Documents Online Application Form be filled in full. photo be added to application form. If there is a Language Exam Result Document (TOEFL-IBT, TOEFL-CBT, TOEFL-PBT, YDS, YÖKDIL IELTS exams accepted by university) can be uploaded to system. (YDS YÖK language documents will be evaluated by considering validity of 5 years validity date of other examination documents is 2 years.) Disabled upload disability document to system. Veteran upload veteran’s certificate to system. Personnel has relative martyrs or veterans upload document proving that they relatives of martyrs or veterans. If has finished a step of higher education level on a foreign language, it be documented. Minimum Requirements In order to be able to participate in activity, first meet following minimum requirements: Working full time in IZU (Academic, administrative) not obligated teaching (Such as research assistants) Assessment Criteria assessment criteria weighted scores to be used in assessing applications are: Mobility Training with Program Countries Evaluation Criteria Criteria No Criteria Description Criteria Rating 1 Benefiting program first time +5 2 Administrative +3 3 wants to benefit program second time during same project period -20 4 Disabled +10 5 Veteran +10 6 is relative of a martyrs or veteran +10 7 If has benefited program during previous project period -10 8 If has benefited program during two previous project period -7 9 If there is an invitation letter institution to be visited +5 10 Those gave up their right to participate on previous project without showing a compelling reason -10 11 Certifying that a high level of education is completed in a foreign language +5 12 administrative certifying result of examination such as YÖKDIL, YDS, TOEFL, IELTS or equivalence examination accepted by OSYM will be awarded with points as indicated in table on side. Score Range Given Points 50-60 +2 61-70 +4 71-80 +6 81-90 +8 91-100 +10 If there is an equality in sort, following priorities applied in order until equality breaks down. Priority 1 Service period in Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (in years). Priority 2 have certified Foreign Language Adequacy. Online applications ranked according to above measures.Candidates with highest score selected as noble, spare ungranted within quotas. Mobility Option with amount of daily allowance to be provided to personnel will benefit is determined by National Agency in accordance with living standards of countries as follows: Country Groups Country Daily Amount Group 1 Denmark, Ireland, Netherlands, Sweden, United Kingdom 144 € Group 2 Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Finland, France, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Norway, Poland, Romania, Turkey* 126 € Group 3 Macedonia, Germany, Latvia, Malta, Portugal, Slovak Republic, Spain 108 € Group 4 Croatia, Estonia, Lithuania, Slovenia 90 € 80% of Erasmus grant is given before starts. remaining 20% is credited to Euro account specified by beneficiary in contract signed with personnel will be able to take advantage of after transactions completed. 2017-2018 Academic year grant is activities between 4 April 2018 - 25 May 2018. willing can take advantage of without grant. amount of grant awarded in is calculated by sending institution in framework of rules determined by National Agency. A person benefits of will be paid a travel allowance based on daily / weekly per diem travel distance within rules set by Center. This payment is a contribution to travel per diem expenses incurred abroad. Payment of travel expenses a contribution to cost of travel to place where participants located. Travel distance is calculated using distance calculator provided by European Commission. Once, it is added to participant's grant. Obtained "km" value Amount of grant 100-499 km 180 € 500-1999 km 275 € 2000-2999 km 360 € 3000-3999 km 530 € 4000-7999 km 820 € Above 8000 km 1.100 € grant given to benefiting of is not intended to cover all expenses related to travel abroad. Mobility Option without may exercise this right by using "without Grant" preference on online form at time of application or by notifying Erasmus Institution Coordinator after results announced. Additional Special Needs Owners For disabled staff, they may receive additional grants according to their specific needs. For detailed information, please contact with Erasmus Office. Quotas Total Quota Number With 3 Spare – With 1 Without Optional Calculation In mobility, selected will be given 5-day grants travel support. Eg: 5 x amount of daily grant of Country of Departure + travel grant given by value of country calculated according to destination country.
